Spaghetti Bolognese.title: Spaghetti Bolognese
method_steps: Finely dice the onion, carrot and celery, and crush the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, celery and garlic for 6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened.
Add the beef mince, breaking it up with a spoon, and brown for 8 minutes until no pink remains.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, then add the tomato passata, tinned tomatoes, beef stock, bay leaves, dried oregano and Worcestershire sauce.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat and cook uncovered for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and coats the back of a spoon.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the spaghetti according to packet instructions until al dente.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving a splash of the cooking water to loosen the sauce if needed.
Remove the bay leaves, then season the bolognese with salt and pepper to taste.
Divide the spaghetti among bowls, top with the bolognese sauce and finish with grated parmesan to serve.

Beef Tacos.title: Beef Tacos
method_steps: Finely dice the onion, shred the lettuce and dice the tomato.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the onion for 3 minutes until softened.
Add the beef mince and brown for 6 minutes, breaking it up as it cooks, until no pink remains.
Stir in the ground cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and cayenne pepper, and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste and 100 ml water, scraping up any browned bits from the base of the pan, and simmer for 5 minutes until thickened.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or microwave until soft and pliable.
Fill each tortilla with the beef mixture, then top with grated cheese, shredded lettuce and diced tomato.
Serve the tacos with lime wedges, while warm.

Beef Chilli con Carne.title: Beef Chilli con Carne
method_steps: Finely dice the onion and crush the garlic.
Heat a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion and garlic for 4 minutes until softened.
Add the beef mince and brown, breaking it up with a spoon, until no pink remains.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, dried oregano, garlic powder and chilli powder, and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste, tinned tomatoes, drained kidney beans and beef stock, and stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat and cook uncovered for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Season the chilli with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the chilli spooned over the rice.

Slow-Cooked Beef Ragu.title: Slow-Cooked Beef Ragu
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into large chunks, finely dice the onion, carrot and celery, and crush the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based pot over medium-high heat and brown the beef in batches, about 3-4 minutes per side, until deeply coloured. Remove and set aside.
Add the onion, carrot, celery and garlic to the pot and cook for 6 minutes, stirring, until softened.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, then return the beef to the pot with the tomato passata, beef stock and bay leaves.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover and cook gently for 2.5-3 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is fork-tender and shreds easily.
Remove the bay leaves and shred the beef through the sauce using two forks, then season with salt and pepper.
Meanwhile, cook the pappardelle in a large pot of salted boiling water according to packet instructions until al dente, then drain.
Toss the pappardelle through the ragu.
Serve topped with grated parmesan.

Beef & Broccoli Stir Fry.title: Beef & Broccoli Stir Fry
method_steps: Cut the broccoli into small florets, thinly slice the carrot, crush the garlic and grate the ginger.
Toss the beef strips with the cornflour until evenly coated.
Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until shimmering and stir-fry the beef for 3 minutes until browned but not fully cooked through, then remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil to the wok with the garlic and ginger, then stir-fry the broccoli and carrot for 4 minutes until bright and just tender-crisp.
Return the beef to the wok along with any resting juices.
Add the soy sauce, oyster sauce and sesame oil, and toss for 1-2 minutes until the beef is cooked through and coated in the sauce.
Serve immediately over the steamed rice.

Beef & Vegetable Stir Fry with Noodles.title: Beef & Vegetable Stir Fry with Noodles
method_steps: Thinly slice the capsicum and carrot, cut the broccoli into small florets, crush the garlic and grate the ginger.
Loosen the hokkien noodles under warm water or according to packet instructions, then set aside.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until shimmering and stir-fry the beef strips for 3 minutes until browned, then remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil with the garlic and ginger, then stir-fry the capsicum, broccoli and carrot for 4 minutes until just tender-crisp.
Return the beef to the wok and add the noodles, oyster sauce, soy sauce and sesame oil.
Toss everything together over high heat for 2 minutes until the noodles are heated through and well coated.
Serve immediately while hot.

Beef Meatballs in Tomato Sauce.title: Beef Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
method_steps: In a large bowl, combine the beef mince, breadcrumbs, egg, parmesan and half the crushed garlic, and mix with your hands until just combined (avoid overworking).
Season the mixture with salt and pepper, then roll into 16-20 even meatballs.

Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat and brown the meatballs in batches for about 5 minutes, turning occasionally, until coloured all over (they don't need to be cooked through yet). Remove and set aside.
Finely dice the onion, then cook with the remaining garlic in the same pan for 4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tomato passata and dried oregano, and bring to a simmer.
Return the meatballs to the sauce, cover and simmer for 15 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through and reaching 75°C in the centre.
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti in a large pot of salted boiling water according to packet instructions until al dente, then drain.
Serve the meatballs and sauce spooned over the spaghetti, scattered with fresh parsley.

Beef Nachos.title: Beef Nachos
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and finely dice the onion.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat and brown the beef mince with the onion for about 6-7 minutes, breaking up the mince, until no pink remains.
Stir in the ground cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and cayenne pepper, and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tinned tomatoes and drained kidney beans, and simmer for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
Spread the corn chips over a large baking tray in an even layer.
Spoon the beef mixture over the corn chips, then scatter the grated cheese on top.
Bake for 8 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling.
Top with dollops of sour cream, sliced jalapeños and spring onion, and serve immediately while hot.

Cottage Pie.title: Cottage Pie
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C. Peel and chop the potatoes, then finely dice the onion and carrot, and crush the garlic.
Boil the potatoes in salted water for 15-20 minutes until tender, then drain and mash with the milk and butter until smooth. Season to taste.
Meanwhile, heat a large frying pan over medium heat and brown the beef mince with the onion, carrot and garlic for about 8 minutes, breaking up the mince, until no pink remains.
Stir in the frozen peas, beef stock, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce and dried thyme.
Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has reduced and the mixture is thick.
Season the mince mixture with salt and pepper, then spoon into a baking dish and spread level.
Top with the mashed potato, spreading to the edges to seal in the filling, and rough up the surface with a fork.
Bake for 25 minutes until the top is golden and the filling is bubbling at the edges.
Rest for a few minutes before serving.

Beef Stroganoff.title: Beef Stroganoff
method_steps: Thinly slice the onion and mushrooms, and crush the garlic.
Heat half the butter in a large frying pan over high heat and brown the beef strips for 2-3 minutes until seared but not fully cooked. Remove and set aside.
Melt the remaining butter in the same pan and cook the onion, mushrooms and garlic for 5 minutes until softened and golden.
Stir in the sweet paprika and cook for 30 seconds, then add the beef stock and Dijon mustard, scraping up any browned bits, and simmer for 5 minutes until slightly reduced.
Reduce the heat to low and stir in the sour cream until smooth (do not let it boil).
Return the beef and any resting juices to the pan and warm through for 1-2 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the fettuccine in a large pot of salted boiling water according to packet instructions until al dente, then drain.
Serve the stroganoff over the fettuccine, scattered with fresh parsley.

Beef Rissoles with Gravy.title: Beef Rissoles with Gravy
method_steps: Peel and chop the potatoes, then boil in salted water for 15-20 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, grate the onion and crush the garlic, then combine in a bowl with the beef mince, breadcrumbs, egg, Worcestershire sauce and parsley. Season with salt and pepper and mix well.
Shape the mixture into 8 even patties.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat and fry the rissoles for 4 minutes on each side until browned and cooked through, reaching 75°C in the centre. Remove and keep warm.
Add the beef stock to the same pan, scraping up the browned bits, and simmer for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened into a gravy.
Steam or boil the peas for the last few minutes until tender.
Drain and mash the potatoes.
Serve the rissoles with the mash and peas, spooning the gravy over the top.

Massaman Beef Curry.title: Massaman Beef Curry
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into large chunks, peel and chop the potatoes into large pieces, and roughly chop the onion.
Heat a large heavy-based pot over medium-high heat and brown the beef in batches for 3-4 minutes per side until well coloured.
Add the onion and massaman curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring, until fragrant.
Pour in the coconut milk, beef stock, potatoes, fish sauce, brown sugar and cinnamon stick, and stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover and cook gently for 1.5-2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is tender and the potatoes are cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions and roughly chop the peanuts.
Remove the cinnamon stick and taste the curry, seasoning with salt if needed.
Serve the curry over rice, scattered with the chopped peanuts.

Beef Burgers with Chips.title: Beef Burgers with Chips
method_steps: Preheat the oven according to the oven chips packet instructions and spread the chips on a baking tray.
Bake the chips for the time stated on the packet, turning halfway through, until golden and crisp.
Meanwhile, shred the lettuce and slice the tomato.
Grate the onion and crush the garlic, then combine in a bowl with the beef mince and Worcestershire sauce. Divide into 4 portions, shape into patties and season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat and fry the patties for 4 minutes on each side until well browned and cooked through, reaching 75°C in the centre.
In the last minute of cooking, top each patty with grated cheese and cover the pan briefly to melt.
Split and lightly toast the burger buns.
Build the burgers with the patties, lettuce, tomato, pickles and tomato sauce, and serve with the hot chips.

Mongolian Beef.title: Mongolian Beef
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Crush the garlic, grate the ginger and thinly slice the spring onions.
Toss the beef strips in the cornflour until evenly coated.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat and fry the beef in batches for 2-3 minutes until crisp and browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, ginger and sesame oil to the wok and simmer for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and coats the back of a spoon.
Return the beef to the wok and toss to coat evenly in the sauce.
Serve immediately over the rice, scattered with the sliced spring onion.

Corned Beef with White Sauce.title: Corned Beef with White Sauce
method_steps: Place the corned silverside in a large pot with the bay leaves, peppercorns and cloves, and cover with cold water.
Add the malt vinegar and brown sugar to the pot, then bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce to low, cover and gently simmer for 2.5 hours, skimming any foam from the surface, until the beef is tender.
About 25 minutes before the beef is ready, peel and chop the potatoes and carrots into even chunks.
Boil the potatoes and carrots in a separate pot of salted water for 15-20 minutes until tender, then drain.
Meanwhile, melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat, whisk in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute to form a paste.
Gradually whisk in the milk until smooth, then simmer for 5 minutes, whisking often, until the sauce coats the back of a spoon. Season with salt and pepper.
Remove the beef from the pot and rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing.
Serve the sliced beef with the boiled potatoes and carrots, spooning the white sauce over the top and scattering with parsley.

Beef Fried Rice.title: Beef Fried Rice
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice ahead of time and spread on a tray to cool completely (day-old refrigerated rice works best).
Finely dice the carrot, crush the garlic and thinly slice the spring onion.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat and scramble the eggs until just set. Remove and set aside.
Heat the remaining oil in the same wok and brown the beef mince with the garlic for about 5 minutes, breaking it up, until no pink remains.
Add the peas, corn and carrot, and stir-fry for 3 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, and toss with the soy sauce and sesame oil over high heat for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Fold the scrambled egg back through the rice.
Serve immediately while hot, scattered with the sliced spring onion.

Steak with Roast Vegetables.title: Steak with Roast Vegetables
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and peel and chop the pumpkin and potatoes into even chunks.
Toss the pumpkin and potato with half the olive oil on a baking tray and season with salt and pepper.
Roast for 20 minutes, then add the green beans to the tray and roast for a further 5 minutes until the vegetables are golden and tender.
Meanwhile, remove the steaks from the fridge, pat dry and season both sides generously with salt and pepper.
Heat the remaining olive oil in a heavy-based frying pan over high heat until very hot.
Pan-sear the steaks for 3 minutes on each side for medium, or until a meat thermometer reads about 60°C for medium-rare, adjusting the time to your preferred doneness.
In the last minute of cooking, add the butter, garlic and rosemary to the pan and baste the steaks with the foaming butter.
Rest the steaks on a plate, loosely covered with foil, for 5 minutes to let the juices redistribute.
Slice the steaks against the grain and serve with the roasted vegetables.

Beef Enchiladas.title: Beef Enchiladas
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and finely dice the onion.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat and brown the beef mince with the onion for about 7 minutes, breaking up the mince, until no pink remains.
Stir in the ground cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and cayenne pepper, and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add half the tinned tomatoes and simmer for 3-4 minutes until the mixture is thick enough to hold its shape.
Spoon the beef mixture down the centre of each tortilla, roll up firmly and place seam-side down in a baking dish.
Pour the remaining tinned tomatoes over the rolled tortillas and scatter with the grated cheese.
Bake for 20 minutes until the cheese is melted, golden and bubbling.
Rest for a few minutes, then serve topped with sour cream and fresh coriander.

Butter Chicken.title: Butter Chicken
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillets into bite-sized pieces and combine in a bowl with the yogurt, crushed garlic, grated ginger and garam masala. Marinate for at least 15 minutes (or overnight in the fridge).

Melt the butter in a large saucepan or deep frying pan over medium-high heat and brown the marinated chicken for 5-6 minutes until sealed and lightly coloured (it doesn't need to be cooked through yet).
Stir in the butter chicken paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato passata and coconut milk, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken reaches 75°C and the sauce has thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions and roughly chop the coriander leaves.
Season the sauce with salt to taste.
Serve over rice, scattered with the fresh coriander.

Chicken & Vegetable Fried Rice.title: Chicken & Vegetable Fried Rice
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice ahead of time and spread on a tray to cool completely (day-old refrigerated rice works best).
Dice the chicken breast into small, even pieces, finely dice the carrot, crush the garlic and thinly slice the spring onion.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat and scramble the eggs until just set. Remove and set aside.
Heat the remaining oil in the same wok and cook the diced chicken with the garlic for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden and no longer pink in the centre.
Add the peas, corn and carrot, and stir-fry for 3 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, and toss with the soy sauce and sesame oil for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Fold the scrambled egg back through the rice.
Serve immediately while hot, scattered with the sliced spring onion.

Honey Soy Chicken Drumsticks.title: Honey Soy Chicken Drumsticks
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large baking tray with baking paper.
Whisk together the honey, soy sauce, crushed garlic and grated ginger in a large bowl.
Add the chicken drumsticks and toss until well coated in the marinade.
Arrange the drumsticks in a single layer on the prepared tray.
Bake for 35 minutes, basting with the pan juices halfway through, until caramelised and the chicken reaches 75°C at the thickest part.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Steam the broccoli for 4-5 minutes until just tender.
Rest the drumsticks for a couple of minutes, drizzle with sesame oil, then serve with the rice and broccoli.

Chicken Schnitzel with Chips & Salad.title: Chicken Schnitzel with Chips & Salad
method_steps: Preheat the oven and spread the oven chips on a baking tray, following the packet directions.
Bake the chips, turning once, until golden and crisp.
While the chips cook, slice the chicken breast horizontally into thin cutlets and flatten slightly between sheets of baking paper for even thickness.
Set up three shallow dishes: plain flour in one, beaten eggs in the second, and breadcrumbs in the third.
Coat each piece of chicken in flour, then egg, then press firmly into the breadcrumbs.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and shallow-fry the schnitzels for about 3 minutes each side until golden and cooked through.
Drain the schnitzels on paper towel and season with salt.
Toss the salad leaves and cherry tomatoes with the olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
Serve the schnitzel with the chips and dressed salad.

Chicken Parmigiana.title: Chicken Parmigiana
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and lightly grease a baking dish.
Slice the chicken breast into 4 even portions and flatten slightly between baking paper to an even thickness.
Set up three dishes with the plain flour, beaten eggs and breadcrumbs, then coat each piece of chicken in flour, egg and finally breadcrumbs.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and shallow-fry the chicken for 2-3 minutes each side until golden (it will finish cooking in the oven).
Arrange the schnitzels in the prepared dish, top each with a slice of ham, then spoon over the tomato passata.
Scatter the grated cheese and torn basil leaves evenly over the top.
Bake for 20 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling and the chicken is cooked through.
Meanwhile, steam the green beans for 4-5 minutes until just tender.
Serve the parmigiana hot with the steamed green beans.

Thai Green Chicken Curry.title: Thai Green Chicken Curry
method_steps: Slice the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan or wok over medium heat and fry the green curry paste for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chicken and stir to coat in the paste, cooking for 2-3 minutes until it starts to colour.
Pour in the coconut milk, fish sauce and brown sugar, stirring to combine.
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 10 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Add the capsicum and green beans and simmer for a further 5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Stir through the Thai basil leaves, taste and adjust seasoning with extra fish sauce if needed, then serve the curry over the rice.

Chicken Fajitas.title: Chicken Fajitas
method_steps: Combine the ground cumin, sweet paprika, chilli powder, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, salt and pepper in a small bowl to make the spice mix.
Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and toss with the spice mix until evenly coated.
Slice the capsicum and onion into thin strips.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat and stir-fry the chicken for about 5 minutes until cooked through and lightly charred, then remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil to the pan and stir-fry the capsicum and onion for about 4 minutes until softened and lightly charred.
Return the chicken to the pan and toss for a minute to combine and heat through.
Warm the tortillas following the packet directions.
Squeeze over the lime juice.
Serve the chicken and vegetables in the warm tortillas with sour cream, grated cheese and coriander, with extra lime wedges.

Roast Chicken with Vegetables.title: Roast Chicken with Vegetables
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Pat the whole chicken dry with paper towel and season generously inside and out with salt and pepper.
Rub the chicken all over with the olive oil, then place the garlic cloves, lemon halves and thyme sprigs inside the cavity.
Place the chicken in a large roasting pan.
Cut the potato, carrot and pumpkin into even chunks and scatter around the chicken.
Roast for 75-80 minutes, turning the vegetables once or twice, until golden and tender and the chicken reaches 75°C at the thickest part of the thigh.
Remove from the oven and rest the chicken for 10 minutes, loosely covered with foil.
Carve the chicken and serve with the roasted vegetables and pan juices.

Chicken & Vegetable Soup.title: Chicken & Vegetable Soup
method_steps: Dice the onion, carrot and celery, crush the garlic, and cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, celery and garlic for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened.
Add the chicken and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring, until lightly browned.
Pour in the chicken stock, add the bay leaf and bring to the boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer, partially covered, for 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the small soup pasta and cook for a further 8-10 minutes, or until the pasta is tender.
Remove the bay leaf and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls, scatter with chopped parsley and serve hot.

Chicken & Chorizo Rice.title: Chicken & Chorizo Rice
method_steps: Dice the onion and capsicum, crush the garlic, cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces and slice the chorizo.
Heat a large, deep frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the chorizo for 2-3 minutes until it releases its oil and turns golden.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook for 4-5 minutes until browned on the outside.
Add the onion, capsicum and garlic and cook for about 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the basmati rice and smoked paprika, coating the rice in the flavoured oil.
Add the tinned tomatoes and chicken stock, stir to combine, and bring to the boil.
Cover, reduce the heat to low and simmer for 18 minutes.
Stir through the frozen peas and cook, covered, for a further 2 minutes until the rice is tender and the peas are heated through.
Remove from the heat, rest covered for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork and serve.

Chicken Satay Skewers.title: Chicken Satay Skewers
method_steps: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 20 minutes to stop them burning.
Cut the chicken thigh fillet into strips and toss with half the soy sauce, the ground coriander and turmeric, then thread onto the skewers.
Preheat a barbecue or chargrill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill the skewers for about 10 minutes, turning occasionally, until charred and the chicken reaches 75°C and is cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.

In a small saucepan, combine the peanut butter, coconut milk, remaining soy sauce, crushed garlic, lime juice and brown sugar and warm over low heat, stirring, until smooth and the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Slice the cucumber.
Serve the skewers over rice with the satay sauce spooned over and cucumber on the side.

Teriyaki Chicken with Rice.title: Teriyaki Chicken with Rice
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Slice the chicken thigh fillet into strips.
Heat a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat and pan-fry the chicken with the crushed garlic and grated ginger for 5-6 minutes, turning, until golden and cooked through.
Steam or microwave the broccoli for 4-5 minutes until just tender.
Pour the teriyaki sauce into the pan with the chicken and toss for about 2 minutes until glazed and glossy.
Remove from the heat and scatter with sesame seeds and sliced spring onion.
Serve the teriyaki chicken over the rice with the steamed broccoli alongside.

Chicken Cacciatore.title: Chicken Cacciatore
method_steps: Dice the onion and capsicum, slice the mushrooms and crush the garlic.
Heat a large, deep frying pan or casserole dish over medium-high heat and brown the chicken thigh cutlets for about 4 minutes each side until golden, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion, garlic, capsicum and mushrooms to the pan and cook for about 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tinned tomatoes, olives, chicken stock, dried oregano and bay leaf, scraping up any browned bits from the base of the pan.
Return the chicken to the pan, nestling it into the sauce.
Cover and simmer gently for 30 minutes, until the chicken is tender and cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the bay leaf and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve the cacciatore with crusty bread or soft polenta.

Chicken Korma.title: Chicken Korma
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces and finely dice the onion.
Heat a large frying pan or saucepan over medium-high heat and cook the onion for about 5 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chicken and brown for about 5 minutes, turning, until coloured on all sides.
Stir in the korma paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the thickened cream and chicken stock, stirring to combine.
Bring to a gentle simmer, stir in the ground almonds, and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is tender and the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Season the korma to taste and serve over the rice.

Sweet & Sour Chicken.title: Sweet & Sour Chicken
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces and toss in the cornflour until evenly coated.
Heat a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat and fry the chicken for 5-6 minutes, turning, until golden and cooked through, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion and capsicum to the pan and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until just softened.
Add the drained tinned pineapple and the chicken back to the pan.
Pour in the sweet & sour sauce and toss everything together for about 3 minutes until well coated and heated through.
Serve the sweet & sour chicken over the rice.

Chicken Burrito Bowls.title: Chicken Burrito Bowls
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Combine the ground cumin, sweet paprika, chilli powder, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and salt in a small bowl.
Cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces and toss with the spice mix until evenly coated.
Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat and pan-fry the chicken for 6-8 minutes, turning, until golden and cooked through.
Meanwhile, warm the tinned black beans and tinned corn in a small saucepan over low heat, or in the microwave.
Divide the rice between bowls and top with the chicken, black beans and corn.
Scatter over the grated cheese, spoon over the salsa and scatter with coriander.
Squeeze over the lime juice and serve immediately.

Lemon & Herb Roast Chicken Pieces.title: Lemon & Herb Roast Chicken Pieces
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Zest and juice the lemon, and crush the garlic.
In a large bowl, combine the lemon zest and juice, garlic, dried oregano and olive oil.
Add the chicken thigh cutlets and toss to coat well in the marinade, seasoning with salt and pepper.
Cut the zucchini and capsicum into chunks.
Arrange the chicken and vegetables in a single layer in a large roasting dish.
Roast for 40 minutes, turning the vegetables once, until the chicken is golden and cooked through (juices run clear) and the vegetables are tender.
Serve straight from the dish.

Chicken Noodle Stir Fry.title: Chicken Noodle Stir Fry
method_steps: Cook the Hokkien noodles according to packet directions, then drain and set aside.
Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and crush the garlic and ginger.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a large wok over high heat and stir-fry the chicken, garlic and ginger for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through, then remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil and stir-fry the mixed vegetables for about 4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return the chicken to the wok.
Add the noodles, hoisin sauce and soy sauce and toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Serve the stir-fry immediately while hot.

Chicken Tikka with Rice.title: Chicken Tikka with Rice
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces and combine with the natural yoghurt, tikka spice, crushed garlic and grated ginger in a bowl; marinate for at least 15 minutes if time allows.
Cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Heat a large frying pan or chargrill pan over medium-high heat and grill or pan-fry the marinated chicken for about 8-10 minutes, turning, until charred in spots and cooked through.
Remove the chicken and set aside.
In the same pan, pour in the tomato passata and simmer gently for about 5 minutes, stirring to lift any flavour from the base of the pan, until slightly thickened.
Stir in the thickened cream and simmer for 2 minutes.
Fold the chicken back through the sauce and simmer for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Season to taste, scatter with fresh coriander, then serve the chicken tikka over the rice.

Chicken Pad Thai.title: Chicken Pad Thai
method_steps: Soak the rice noodles in hot water according to packet directions until softened, then drain.
Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and crush the garlic.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat and stir-fry the chicken and garlic for about 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Push the chicken to one side of the wok, crack in the eggs and scramble for a minute or two until just set, then mix through the chicken.
Add the drained noodles and pad thai sauce and toss everything together for 2-3 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Roughly chop the peanuts and slice the spring onion.
Divide the pad thai between bowls and top with the peanuts, bean sprouts and spring onion.
Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

Chicken & Leek Pie.title: Chicken & Leek Pie
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces and slice the leek.
Melt the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the chicken, leek and thyme for 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is lightly coloured and the leek has softened.
Stir in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to cook out the raw flour taste.
Gradually pour in the chicken stock, stirring continuously to avoid lumps, then stir in the thickened cream.
Simmer gently for 5 minutes, stirring, until the sauce has thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon.
Season to taste, then pour the filling into a pie dish.
Top with the puff pastry, trimming any excess and pressing to seal the edges, then brush with the beaten egg.
Bake for 25 minutes until the pastry is puffed and golden brown.

Tandoori Chicken with Rice.title: Tandoori Chicken with Rice
method_steps: Combine the natural yoghurt, tandoori spice, crushed garlic, grated ginger and lemon juice in a large bowl.
Add the chicken thigh cutlets and turn to coat thoroughly in the marinade; marinate for at least 15 minutes, longer if time allows.
Preheat the oven to 220°C and line a baking tray.
Arrange the marinated chicken on the tray in a single layer.
Bake for 25 minutes, turning once, until the chicken is charred in spots and cooked through (juices run clear).
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet directions.
Slice the cucumber and combine with a little extra yoghurt and chopped mint to make a quick raita.
Serve the tandoori chicken with the rice and cucumber raita.

Chicken Laksa.title: Chicken Laksa
method_steps: Slice the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized strips.
Heat a splash of oil in a large pot over medium heat and fry the laksa paste for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in the coconut milk and chicken stock, stir in the fish sauce and brown sugar, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the sliced chicken and simmer for 8-10 minutes until cooked through and no longer pink in the centre.
Meanwhile, cook the rice noodles according to the packet instructions and drain.
Add the bean sprouts to the soup and cook for 1 minute until just wilted.
Divide the noodles between bowls and ladle over the hot laksa and chicken.
Scatter with fresh coriander and serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

Chicken Caesar Salad.title: Chicken Caesar Salad
method_steps: Preheat a griddle pan or grill to medium-high heat.
Rub the chicken breast with a little olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the chicken for 6-7 minutes each side until golden and cooked through (internal temperature 75°C).
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes, then slice.
Cube the bread, toss in the remaining olive oil and toast in a dry pan until golden for croutons.
Tear the cos lettuce into a large bowl and toss with the caesar dressing.
Top the salad with the sliced chicken, croutons and grated parmesan.
Serve immediately while the chicken is warm.

Honey Mustard Chicken Traybake.title: Honey Mustard Chicken Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Chop the potato and carrot into even-sized chunks.
Whisk the honey, wholegrain mustard, garlic, thyme and olive oil together in a large bowl.
Add the chicken thigh cutlets and vegetables to the bowl and toss until well coated.
Spread everything in a single layer on a large baking tray.
Roast for 40 minutes, turning once halfway, until the chicken is golden and cooked through (75°C) and the vegetables are tender.
Rest the chicken for a few minutes before serving.
Serve straight from the tray, spooning any pan juices over the top.

Sausage & Vegetable Tray Bake.title: Sausage & Vegetable Tray Bake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Chop the potatoes, capsicum, red onion and zucchini into even-sized pieces.
Spread the vegetables over a large baking tray, scatter with garlic and dried oregano, and drizzle with the olive oil, tossing to coat.
Nestle the sausages among the vegetables.
Bake for 40 minutes, turning the sausages halfway, until they are golden and cooked through (75°C) and the vegetables are tender.
Rest the sausages for a couple of minutes.
Serve straight from the tray.

Sausages with Mash & Peas.title: Sausages with Mash & Peas
method_steps: Peel and chop the potatoes, then boil in salted water for about 15 minutes until tender.
Drain the potatoes and mash with the milk and butter until smooth, then season to taste.
Meanwhile, heat a frying pan over medium heat and pan-fry the sausages for 12-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned and cooked through (75°C).
Remove the sausages from the pan and rest briefly.

Add the sliced onion to the same pan and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened, then pour in the beef stock, scraping up any browned bits, and simmer for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened into a gravy.
Cook the frozen peas in boiling water or the microwave until tender.
Serve the sausages over the mash with the peas alongside, spooning the onion gravy over the top.

Spaghetti Carbonara.title: Spaghetti Carbonara
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the spaghetti until al dente.
Meanwhile, dice the bacon.
Heat a frying pan over medium heat and fry the bacon for about 5 minutes until crisp.
Whisk the eggs with the parmesan and black pepper in a bowl.
Reserve a cup of pasta water, then drain the pasta.
Remove the pan from the heat and add the drained pasta to the bacon, tossing to combine.
Off the heat, pour in the egg mixture, tossing quickly and adding a splash of the reserved pasta water until the sauce turns glossy and coats the pasta.
Serve immediately with extra parmesan and black pepper.

Pork San Choy Bau.title: Pork San Choy Bau
method_steps: Mince the garlic, grate the carrot, drain and chop the water chestnuts, and slice the spring onion.
Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat and stir-fry the pork mince for 6 minutes, breaking it up, until browned.
Add the garlic, carrot, water chestnuts and half the spring onion, and stir-fry for 3 minutes until softened.
Stir in the sesame oil, hoisin sauce and soy sauce, cooking for 1 minute until glossy.
Separate the iceberg lettuce leaves into cups.
Spoon the pork mixture into the lettuce cups and scatter with the remaining spring onion.
Serve immediately while warm.

Pork Chops with Apple & Greens.title: Pork Chops with Apple & Greens
method_steps: Slice the apples.
Heat the olive oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat and season the pork chops with salt and pepper.
Pan-fry the chops for 4 minutes each side until golden and cooked through (internal temperature 63-65°C).
Remove the chops and rest for 5 minutes, loosely covered with foil.
Add the sliced apple to the same pan and cook for 3 minutes until softened and lightly caramelised.
Steam the green beans and broccolini for about 4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Serve the rested chops with the cooked apple and steamed greens.

Slow-Cooker Pulled Pork.title: Slow-Cooker Pulled Pork
method_steps: Mix the smoked paprika, brown sugar, garlic powder, onion powder and a pinch of salt, then rub all over the pork shoulder.
Place the pork in the slow cooker and pour the barbecue sauce and apple cider vinegar over the top.
Cook on low for about 5 hours until the pork is very tender and shreds easily with a fork.
Meanwhile, shred the cabbage and grate the carrot, tossing together to make a slaw.
Remove the pork and shred it with two forks, then stir it back through the cooking juices.
Split and lightly toast the bread rolls.
Pile the pulled pork into the rolls and top with the slaw.
Serve while the pork is still warm.

Sweet & Sour Pork.title: Sweet & Sour Pork
method_steps: Cut the pork loin into bite-sized pieces and toss in the cornflour to coat.
Cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat and fry the pork for 5-6 minutes until golden and cooked through, then remove.
Add the sliced onion, capsicum and drained pineapple to the pan and stir-fry for 3 minutes until softened.
Return the pork to the pan and pour in the sweet and sour sauce.
Toss and simmer for 2 minutes until the sauce is glossy and coats the pork.
Serve over the steamed rice.

Pork & Vegetable Stir Fry.title: Pork & Vegetable Stir Fry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Cut the broccoli into florets and slice the capsicum and carrot.
Heat the vegetable oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry the pork strips for 3-4 minutes until browned, then remove and set aside.
Add the garlic and ginger to the wok and stir-fry for 30 seconds, then add the broccoli, capsicum and carrot and stir-fry for 4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return the pork to the wok.
Add the soy sauce and oyster sauce, tossing and cooking for 1-2 minutes until the pork is cooked through and everything is coated in sauce.
Serve the stir-fry over the steamed rice.

Pork Schnitzel with Vegetables.title: Pork Schnitzel with Vegetables
method_steps: Set up three shallow dishes: one with the plain flour, one with the beaten egg, and one with the breadcrumbs.
Coat each pork schnitzel steak in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs, pressing gently so the crumbs stick.
Heat a generous layer of oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Shallow-fry the schnitzels for 3 minutes each side until golden and cooked through (internal temperature 75°C).
Drain the schnitzels on paper towel.
Slice the carrots and steam with the peas for about 5 minutes until tender.
Serve the schnitzels with the steamed peas and carrots and lemon wedges for squeezing over.

Sausage Pasta.title: Sausage Pasta
method_steps: Squeeze the sausage meat out of the skins into a large pan, discarding the skins.
Heat the pan over medium heat and brown the sausage meat for about 5 minutes, breaking it up with a spoon.
Add the diced onion, minced garlic, dried oregano and chilli flakes, cooking for 3 minutes until the onion has softened.
Pour in the tomato passata, stir and bring to a simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the penne in salted boiling water until al dente, reserving a little pasta water before draining.
Toss the drained pasta through the sauce, loosening with reserved pasta water if needed.
Serve topped with grated parmesan.

Honey Soy Pork Meatballs.title: Honey Soy Pork Meatballs
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Mix the pork mince, breadcrumbs, minced garlic and ginger in a bowl and season with salt and pepper.
Roll the mixture into meatballs.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat and brown the meatballs all over, about 6-8 minutes.
Add the honey, soy sauce and a splash of water to the pan, stirring to coat the meatballs.
Simmer for 5 minutes, turning occasionally, until the glaze is sticky and the meatballs are cooked through (75°C).
Steam the broccoli until tender.
Serve the meatballs over the rice with the steamed broccoli, scattered with spring onion and with the glaze spooned over the top.

BLT-Style Bacon & Egg Pasta.title: BLT-Style Bacon & Egg Pasta
method_steps: Cook the spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente, reserving a little pasta water before draining.
Meanwhile, dice the bacon and halve the cherry tomatoes.
Heat a pan over medium heat and fry the bacon for about 4 minutes until crisp.
Add the cherry tomatoes and cook for 2-3 minutes until starting to soften.
Add the baby spinach and cook until just wilted.
Beat the eggs in a small bowl with the parmesan and black pepper.
Remove the pan from the heat, toss through the drained pasta, then pour in the egg mixture, tossing quickly until glossy and just set, loosening with reserved pasta water if needed.
Serve immediately.

Shepherd's Pie.title: Shepherd's Pie
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Peel and chop the potatoes, boil in salted water for about 15 minutes until tender, then drain and mash with the milk and butter.
Meanwhile, dice the onion and carrot.
Heat a large pan over medium-high heat and brown the lamb mince for 5-6 minutes, breaking it up.
Add the onion, carrot and garlic, cooking for 4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the Worcestershire sauce, peas and beef stock, then simmer for 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Transfer the mince mixture to a baking dish and spread the mash evenly over the top.
Bake for 25 minutes until golden and bubbling at the edges.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

Lamb Roast with Vegetables.title: Lamb Roast with Vegetables
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Mince the garlic and finely chop the rosemary.
Rub the lamb leg all over with olive oil, garlic and rosemary, then season with salt and pepper.
Chop the potato, pumpkin and carrot into even chunks and toss with a little olive oil.
Place the lamb in a roasting pan and arrange the vegetables around it.
Roast for 80-90 minutes, turning the vegetables once, until the lamb is cooked to your liking (about 65°C for medium) and the vegetables are golden and tender.
Remove the lamb and rest for 15 minutes, loosely covered with foil.
Carve the lamb and serve with the roasted vegetables.

Lamb Kofta with Flatbread.title: Lamb Kofta with Flatbread
method_steps: Preheat a barbecue or grill to medium-high heat.
Mix the lamb mince with the grated onion, ground cumin, ground coriander, minced garlic and chopped parsley, then season.
Shape the mixture around skewers into kofta.
Grill the kofta for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned and cooked through (75°C).
Meanwhile, dice the tomato and cucumber and toss together for a simple salad.
Warm the flatbread.
Serve the kofta in the flatbread with the tomato-cucumber salad and a dollop of natural yoghurt.

Lamb Curry.title: Lamb Curry
method_steps: Cut the lamb shoulder into bite-sized chunks.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat and cook the diced onion for 3-4 minutes until softened, then brown the lamb in batches for 5-6 minutes, and remove.
Add the garlic, ginger and rogan josh paste to the pot and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the lamb to the pot, then add the diced tomatoes and beef stock, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer, cover and reduce the heat, then simmer for 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is tender.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Serve the curry over the rice.

Lamb Chops with Mash.title: Lamb Chops with Mash
method_steps: Peel and chop the potatoes, then boil in salted water for about 15 minutes until tender.
Drain and mash the potatoes with the butter until smooth, then season to taste.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and season the lamb loin chops with salt, pepper, garlic and rosemary.
Pan-fry the chops for 3 minutes each side until browned and cooked to about 63°C for medium.
Rest the chops for 5 minutes.
Steam the green beans for about 4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Serve the chops with the mash and steamed beans.

Moussaka.title: Moussaka
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C and slice the eggplant into 1cm rounds.
Heat half the olive oil in a large frying pan and fry the eggplant in batches until golden on both sides, about 8 minutes; set aside.
Add the remaining oil to the pan with the lamb mince, diced onion and crushed garlic; cook over medium-high heat, breaking up the mince, until browned, about 8 minutes.
Stir in the cinnamon and oregano and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste, cook for 1 minute, then add the diced tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat, whisk in the flour, and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the milk until smooth, then cook, whisking constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes; stir in the nutmeg and most of the parmesan.
Layer half the fried eggplant in a baking dish, top with the lamb mixture, then cover with the remaining eggplant.
Pour the cheese sauce evenly over the top and sprinkle with the reserved parmesan.
Bake for 35-40 minutes until golden and bubbling, then rest for 5 minutes before serving.

Lamb Souvlaki Wraps.title: Lamb Souvlaki Wraps
method_steps: Cut the lamb leg steaks into bite-sized strips.
Combine the lamb with the olive oil, oregano, crushed garlic and juice from half the lemon; toss to coat and marinate for 10 minutes if time allows.
Heat a chargrill pan or barbecue plate over high heat.
Cook the lamb for about 8 minutes, turning occasionally, until well charred outside and just cooked through.
Remove the lamb from the heat and rest for 3 minutes.
Warm the pita breads on the grill or in a dry pan for about 30 seconds each side, until soft and lightly toasted.
Thinly slice the tomato and red onion.
Spread each pita with tzatziki, then fill with the lamb, salad leaves, tomato and red onion.
Squeeze over the remaining lemon juice and serve immediately.

Baked Salmon with Roast Vegetables.title: Baked Salmon with Roast Vegetables
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C and line a large baking tray.
Cut the pumpkin and potatoes into 2-3cm chunks and cut the broccoli into florets.
Toss the pumpkin and potato with half the olive oil, season with salt and pepper, and spread over the tray.
Roast for 15 minutes, then add the broccoli tossed in the remaining oil and roast a further 5 minutes, until the vegetables are tender and golden.
Push the vegetables to one side of the tray, add the salmon fillets, and season with salt and pepper.
Roast for a further 12 minutes, or until the salmon flakes easily with a fork.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the salmon and vegetables.
Serve immediately straight from the tray.

Teriyaki Salmon with Rice.title: Teriyaki Salmon with Rice
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to the packet directions; keep warm.
While the rice cooks, steam or blanch the broccolini for 3-4 minutes until bright green and just tender.
Pat the salmon fillets dry and season lightly with salt.
Heat a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat and sear the salmon skin-side down for 4 minutes, until the skin is crisp.
Flip the salmon and cook for a further 2 minutes, until almost cooked through.
Reduce the heat to low, spoon the teriyaki sauce over the salmon, and turn to coat, letting it bubble into a glaze for 1-2 minutes.
Thinly slice the spring onion.
Serve the salmon over the rice with the broccolini, spooning any extra pan sauce over the top and scattering with spring onion and sesame seeds.

Oven-Baked Fish & Chips.title: Oven-Baked Fish & Chips
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and spread the oven chips over a baking tray.
Bake the chips for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, set up three shallow bowls: one with plain flour, one with the lightly beaten eggs, and one with the breadcrumbs.
Pat the fish fillets dry, then coat each piece in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs, pressing gently so the crumb sticks.
Move the chips to one side of the tray and add the crumbed fish.
Bake for a further 15 minutes, until the fish is golden and cooked through and the chips are crisp.
Meanwhile, cook the frozen peas in boiling water for 3-4 minutes until tender, then drain.
Serve the fish and chips with the peas and lemon wedges.

Baked Barramundi with Salad.title: Baked Barramundi with Salad
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray.
Place the barramundi fillets on the tray, drizzle with half the olive oil and a squeeze of lemon juice, and season with salt and pepper.
Bake for 15-18 minutes, until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
Meanwhile, roughly chop the tomato, cucumber and avocado, and thinly slice the red onion.
Toss the salad leaves with the chopped tomato, cucumber, avocado and red onion in a large bowl.
Dress the salad with the remaining olive oil and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Divide the salad between plates and top with the baked barramundi.
Serve with any remaining lemon wedges.

Tuna Mornay Pasta Bake.title: Tuna Mornay Pasta Bake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and cook the macaroni in boiling salted water until just al dente, then drain.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat, add the diced onion, and cook for 4 minutes until softened.
Whisk in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the milk, a little at a time, until smooth, then cook, whisking constantly, until it thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes.
Remove from the heat and stir in the mustard and most of the grated cheese until melted.
Fold through the drained tuna, corn, peas and cooked pasta.
Transfer to a baking dish and top with the remaining cheese.
Bake for 20 minutes, until golden and bubbling.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

Fish Tacos.title: Fish Tacos
method_steps: Finely shred the cabbage to make a quick slaw.
Combine the cumin, smoked paprika, chilli powder, garlic powder and oregano in a small bowl.
Pat the fish fillets dry, cut into strips if large, and toss with the spice mix to coat evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
Cook the fish for 3 minutes on each side, until golden outside and cooked through and flaking easily.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or microwave for 20-30 seconds, until soft and pliable.
Flake the cooked fish into large pieces.
Fill the tortillas with the fish, cabbage slaw, a dollop of sour cream and the coriander leaves.
Squeeze over the lime juice and serve immediately.

Prawn Stir Fry with Noodles.title: Prawn Stir Fry with Noodles
method_steps: Prepare the hokkien noodles according to the packet directions, then drain and set aside.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until shimmering.
Stir-fry the prawns with the garlic and ginger for 2 minutes, until just turning pink, then remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil and stir-fry the mixed vegetables for 4 minutes, until crisp-tender.
Return the prawns to the wok.
Add the noodles, soy sauce and sesame oil, tossing everything together for 1-2 minutes until heated through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Garlic Prawn Pasta.title: Garlic Prawn Pasta
method_steps: Cook the spaghetti in a large pot of boiling salted water until al dente; reserve a cup of pasta water, then drain.
While the pasta cooks, finely chop the garlic and parsley.
Heat the olive oil and butter in a large frying pan over medium heat, add the garlic and chilli flakes, and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the prawns and fry for about 3 minutes, until pink and just cooked through.
Add the drained spaghetti to the pan with a squeeze of lemon juice and a splash of the reserved pasta water.
Toss well to coat, adding a little more pasta water if the sauce needs loosening.
Stir through the chopped parsley.
Serve immediately with extra lemon wedges on the side.

Fish Curry.title: Fish Curry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet directions; keep warm.
Cut the fish fillets into large chunks.
Heat a splash of oil in a saucepan over medium heat, add the diced onion, and cook for 4 minutes until softened.
Add the curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk, stir to combine, and bring to a gentle simmer, then simmer for 5 minutes to let the flavours develop.
Add the fish pieces and cook gently for 8 minutes, until just cooked through and flaking easily — keep at a low simmer rather than a hard boil so the fish stays tender.
Stir through the baby spinach until wilted.
Squeeze in the lime juice and serve over the rice.

Salmon & Pea Pasta.title: Salmon & Pea Pasta
method_steps: Cook the penne in a large pot of boiling salted water, adding the frozen peas for the last 2 minutes; reserve a cup of pasta water, then drain.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat and pan-fry the salmon fillets for 4-5 minutes each side, depending on thickness, until just cooked through.
Add the crushed garlic to the pan for the final minute of cooking, until fragrant.
Flake the cooked salmon into large chunks, discarding the skin.
Return the drained pasta and peas to the pot over low heat.
Add the thickened cream and a squeeze of lemon juice, stirring to coat, and loosen with a splash of the reserved pasta water if needed.
Gently fold through the flaked salmon and chopped dill, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

Prawn Fried Rice.title: Prawn Fried Rice
method_steps: If not using leftover rice, cook the basmati rice ahead of time and spread on a tray to cool completely, as cold rice fries best.
Finely dice the carrot, crush the garlic, and thinly slice the spring onion.
Heat a little oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat, pour in the beaten eggs, and scramble until just set; remove and set aside.
Add a little more oil to the wok and stir-fry the prawns over high heat for 2 minutes, until pink and just cooked through.
Add the garlic, peas, corn and carrot, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until tender.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Pour in the soy sauce and sesame oil and toss to coat evenly.
Fold the scrambled egg and spring onion back through the rice.
Serve immediately while hot.

Fish Pie.title: Fish Pie
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and peel and chop the potatoes for mashing.
Boil the potatoes in salted water for 15-20 minutes until tender, then drain and mash with a third of the butter and a splash of milk.
Meanwhile, thinly slice the leek.

Place the fish fillets in a wide pan with the leek, cover with the milk, and gently poach over low heat for 8-10 minutes until just cooked through; remove the fish and leek, reserving the poaching milk.
Melt the remaining butter in a saucepan over medium heat, whisk in the flour, and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the reserved poaching milk until smooth, then cook, whisking constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes; stir in the mustard.
Flake the fish into large chunks and fold through the sauce along with the leek and frozen peas.
Season to taste and spoon the mixture into a baking dish.
Top with the mashed potato and cheddar, spreading it to the edges to seal in the filling.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden and bubbling at the edges, rest for 5 minutes, and scatter with parsley before serving.

Vegetable & Lentil Curry.title: Vegetable & Lentil Curry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet directions; keep warm.
Dice the onion and carrot, and crush the garlic.
Heat a little oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion, garlic and carrot for 5 minutes, until softened.
Stir in the curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the drained lentils, diced tomatoes, coconut milk and a splash of water, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the carrot is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Stir through the baby spinach until wilted.
Season to taste.
Serve over the rice, scattered with coriander leaves.

Pumpkin & Chickpea Curry.title: Pumpkin & Chickpea Curry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet directions; keep warm.
Dice the onion, peel and cut the pumpkin into 2cm chunks, and finely grate the ginger.
Heat a little oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion, garlic and ginger for 4-5 minutes, until softened.
Stir in the curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the pumpkin, tossing to coat in the paste.
Add the drained chickpeas, coconut milk and a splash of water, stirring to combine.
Simmer for 20 minutes, until the pumpkin is tender when pierced with a knife.
Stir through the baby spinach until wilted.
Serve over the rice, scattered with coriander leaves.

Vegetable Stir-Fry with Tofu.title: Vegetable Stir-Fry with Tofu
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet directions; keep warm.
Pat the tofu dry and cut into 2cm cubes; slice the capsicum and carrot, cut the broccoli into small florets, and crush the garlic and grate the ginger.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat and fry the tofu, turning occasionally, until golden on all sides, about 5 minutes; remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil and stir-fry the capsicum, broccoli, carrot, garlic and ginger for 4 minutes, until crisp-tender.
Return the tofu to the wok.
Add the soy sauce, hoisin sauce and sesame oil, tossing to coat everything evenly.
Cook for a further 1 minute until heated through and glossy.
Serve over the rice.

Minestrone Soup.title: Minestrone Soup
method_steps: Finely dice the onion, carrot, celery and zucchini, and crush the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, celery, zucchini and garlic for 6 minutes, until softened.
Stir in the dried oregano and basil and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes and vegetable stock, and stir in the drained cannellini beans.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
Add the pasta and cook for a further 8-10 minutes, or until al dente.
Stir through the baby spinach until wilted.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls, top with parmesan if using, and serve with crusty bread.

Vegetable Fried Rice.title: Vegetable Fried Rice
method_steps: If not using leftover rice, cook the basmati rice ahead and spread on a tray to cool completely, as cold rice fries best.
Lightly beat the eggs in a small bowl, crush the garlic, and thinly slice the spring onion.
Heat half the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat, pour in the eggs, and scramble until just set; remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil and stir-fry the frozen mixed vegetables and garlic for 4 minutes, until tender.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps with a spoon or spatula.
Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until the rice is heated through and starting to catch slightly at the edges.
Pour in the soy sauce and sesame oil and toss to coat evenly.
Fold the scrambled egg and spring onion back through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Red Lentil Dahl.title: Red Lentil Dahl
method_steps: Rinse the red lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
Dice the onion, crush the garlic, and finely grate the ginger.
Heat the ghee in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion, garlic and ginger for 5 minutes, until softened and fragrant.
Stir in the turmeric, cumin and garam masala and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the red lentils, diced tomatoes and enough water to cover, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are soft and the dahl has thickened to a creamy consistency.
Season to taste with salt.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet directions.
Serve the dahl over the rice, scattered with coriander leaves.

Vegetable Lasagne.title: Vegetable Lasagne
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C and finely dice the onion, carrot, zucchini and button mushrooms, and mince the garlic.
Heat a little oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, zucchini and mushrooms, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 6-8 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, then pour in the tomato passata, stir in the dried oregano, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Meanwhile, melt the butter in a separate saucepan over medium heat, whisk in the plain flour and cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the milk until smooth, then simmer, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon, about 5-6 minutes; season with a pinch of nutmeg.
Stir half the mozzarella and the parmesan into the white sauce until melted.

Spread a little vegetable sauce over the base of a baking dish, then layer lasagne sheets, vegetable sauce and white sauce, repeating until everything is used, finishing with a layer of white sauce on top.
Scatter the remaining mozzarella over the top.
Bake for 35 minutes until the top is golden, the sauce is bubbling and a knife slides easily through the pasta.
Rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing and serving.

Macaroni Cheese.title: Macaroni Cheese
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the macaroni until al dente, about 9-10 minutes, then drain.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk in the plain flour and cook, stirring, for 1 minute to form a smooth paste.
Gradually whisk in the milk, a little at a time, until fully smooth.
Simmer the sauce, stirring often, until it thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes, then stir in the Dijon mustard.
Remove from the heat and stir in most of the grated cheese until melted, then season with salt and pepper.
Fold the drained macaroni through the cheese sauce and tip into a baking or ovenproof dish, then scatter over the remaining cheese and breadcrumbs.
Grill until the top is golden and bubbling, about 3-5 minutes, and serve immediately.

Shakshuka.title: Shakshuka
method_steps: Finely dice the onion and red capsicum, and mince the garlic.
Heat a little oil in a large frying pan with a lid over medium heat and cook the onion and capsicum, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, smoked paprika and chilli flakes and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour in the tinned tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Make 6 small wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each one.
Cover the pan and cook over low heat until the egg whites are set but the yolks are still soft, about 6-8 minutes.
Scatter the crumbled feta and parsley over the top.
Rest for a minute, then serve straight from the pan with warm crusty bread for scooping.

Vegetable Frittata.title: Vegetable Frittata
method_steps: Preheat the grill and peel and thinly slice the potatoes, then dice the zucchini and onion.
Heat a little oil in an ovenproof frying pan over medium heat and cook the potato for 5 minutes to start softening.
Add the zucchini and onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until all the vegetables are soft and lightly golden, about 8-10 minutes.
Meanwhile, whisk the eggs with the milk and most of the grated cheese and season with salt and pepper.
Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables in the pan, tilting to distribute evenly.
Cook over low-medium heat, without stirring, until the edges are set and the frittata is mostly firm, about 8-10 minutes.
Scatter the remaining cheese over the top and place under the grill until golden and just set, about 3-4 minutes.
Rest for 5 minutes, scatter with chives, then slice into wedges and serve.

Halloumi & Vegetable Traybake.title: Halloumi & Vegetable Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and chop the capsicum, zucchini and red onion into bite-sized chunks, then drain and rinse the chickpeas.
Toss the vegetables and chickpeas with the olive oil and dried oregano on a large baking tray and season well.
Roast for 25 minutes until the vegetables are tender and starting to catch colour at the edges.
Meanwhile, slice the halloumi.
Remove the tray and nestle the halloumi slices among the vegetables.
Return to the oven and roast for a further 8 minutes until the halloumi is golden and the vegetables are caramelised.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the tray.
Serve straight from the tray while hot.

Vegetarian Burritos.title: Vegetarian Burritos
method_steps: Rinse the rice and cook according to the packet instructions until tender, about 12 minutes, then drain the black beans and corn.
Combine the cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and chilli powder in a small bowl.
Warm the black beans, corn and spice mix in a saucepan, simmering for 5 minutes until heated through and fragrant.
Warm the tortillas briefly in a dry pan or the microwave to make them pliable.
Lay out each tortilla and spoon rice, the bean mixture, grated cheese, salsa and coriander into the centre.
Fold in the sides of the tortilla and roll up tightly to enclose the filling.
Toast the burritos seam-side down in a dry or lightly oiled pan over medium heat until golden and the cheese has melted, 2-3 minutes each side.
Slice in half and serve warm with lime wedges.

Pumpkin Risotto.title: Pumpkin Risotto
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C, dice the pumpkin, toss with a little oil and roast for 20-25 minutes until tender and golden.
Meanwhile, finely dice the onion and mince the garlic, then heat the vegetable stock in a saucepan, keeping it at a gentle simmer.
Heat a little oil in a wide, heavy-based pan over medium heat and cook the onion until soft, about 4 minutes, then add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the arborio rice and stir for 1-2 minutes until the grains look glossy and translucent at the edges.
Pour in the white wine, if using, and stir until absorbed.
Add the hot stock a ladleful at a time, stirring frequently and waiting until each addition is absorbed before adding the next, for about 18-20 minutes until the rice is creamy and just tender.
Stir the roasted pumpkin, butter and parmesan through the risotto and season to taste.
Rest for 2 minutes so the sauce thickens to coat the back of a spoon, then serve immediately with extra parmesan and sage.

Bean & Vegetable Chilli.title: Bean & Vegetable Chilli
method_steps: Dice the onion and red capsicum and mince the garlic, then drain and rinse the kidney and black beans.
Heat a little oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion and capsicum, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, chilli powder, smoked paprika and dried oregano and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir in the tinned tomatoes, kidney beans and black beans.
Simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes until thickened to a rich sauce.
Meanwhile, cook the rice according to the packet instructions, about 12 minutes.
Season the chilli to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve the chilli spooned over the rice, scattered with coriander.

Gnocchi with Tomato & Basil.title: Gnocchi with Tomato & Basil
method_steps: Mince the garlic and roughly tear the basil leaves.
Heat a little oil in a large saucepan over low-medium heat and cook the garlic gently until fragrant, about 30 seconds, taking care not to let it brown.
Add the tomato passata and chilli flakes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
Meanwhile, bring a separate pot of salted water to the boil and cook the gnocchi until they float to the surface, about 2-3 minutes, then drain.
Add the drained gnocchi to the sauce and stir gently to coat, then stir in the butter to enrich the sauce.
Stir through the torn basil and half the parmesan.
Serve immediately, topped with the remaining parmesan.

Falafel Wraps.title: Falafel Wraps
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C if baking the falafel, and slice the tomato and cucumber.
Bake the falafel on a tray for 15-18 minutes, turning once, until golden and heated through (or shallow-fry in oil for 3-4 minutes each side until golden).
Warm the pita breads in a dry pan or the oven for 1-2 minutes until soft and pliable.
Spread each pita with a generous layer of hummus.
Fill with the falafel, salad leaves, sliced tomato and cucumber.
Squeeze over a little lemon juice.
Fold the pita around the filling and serve immediately.

Stuffed Capsicums.title: Stuffed Capsicums
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C, then halve the capsicums lengthways and remove the seeds and core.
Finely dice the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat a little oil in a saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion until soft, about 4 minutes, then add the garlic and cook for a further minute.
Stir in the rice, tinned tomatoes, dried oregano and a little water, then cover and simmer according to the rice packet instructions until the rice is tender, about 15 minutes.
Season the rice mixture to taste and spoon into the capsicum halves.
Top each filled half with grated cheese.
Place in a baking dish and bake for 30 minutes until the capsicum is tender and the cheese is golden.
Rest for 5 minutes, scatter with parsley, and serve.

Pea & Mint Pasta.title: Pea & Mint Pasta
method_steps: Zest and juice the lemon, roughly chop the mint leaves and mince the garlic.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the orecchiette according to the packet instructions until almost al dente.
Add the frozen peas to the pasta water for the last 2 minutes of cooking.
Drain the pasta and peas, reserving a cup of the cooking water.
Heat the olive oil in the same pot over low heat and cook the garlic gently for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the pasta and peas to the pot and toss through the lemon zest and juice, mint and parmesan.
Loosen with a splash of the reserved cooking water if the pasta looks dry.
Season to taste and serve immediately with extra parmesan.

Homemade Pizza Night.title: Homemade Pizza Night
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C, placing a tray or pizza stone inside to heat up.
Spread each pizza base with tomato passata, leaving a small border for the crust.
Scatter the dried oregano over the passata.
Scatter the grated mozzarella evenly over both bases.
Top with the sliced ham.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until the base is crisp and the cheese is golden and bubbling.
Meanwhile, toss the mixed salad leaves with a drizzle of olive oil.
Rest the pizzas briefly, then slice and serve with the salad.

Beef & Bacon Pasta Bake.title: Beef & Bacon Pasta Bake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C, dice the onion and bacon, and mince the garlic.
Heat a large pan over medium-high heat and cook the bacon and onion together until the bacon starts to crisp, about 4 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the beef mince, breaking it up with a spoon, and cook until well browned all over, about 6-8 minutes.
Pour in the tomato passata, stir in the dried oregano and basil, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the penne in a large pot of salted boiling water until al dente, then drain.
Fold the drained pasta through the meat sauce until well combined.
Transfer to a baking dish and scatter the grated cheese over the top.
Bake for 20 minutes until golden and bubbling, then rest for 5 minutes before serving.

Chicken & Bacon Alfredo.title: Chicken & Bacon Alfredo
method_steps: Dice the chicken breast and the bacon, and mince the garlic.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the fettuccine according to the packet instructions until al dente, then drain, reserving a little pasta water.
Meanwhile, heat a large pan over medium-high heat and cook the bacon until crisp, then remove and set aside.
Add the chicken to the same pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden and cooked through (internal temperature of 75°C), about 6-8 minutes.
Add the garlic and butter and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the bacon to the pan and pour in the thickened cream, bringing it to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the parmesan until melted and the sauce coats the back of a spoon, about 2-3 minutes.
Toss the drained pasta through the sauce, loosening with a splash of reserved pasta water if needed.
Season to taste, scatter with parsley, and serve immediately with extra parmesan.

Nasi Goreng.title: Nasi Goreng
method_steps: Cook the rice according to the packet instructions, then spread it on a tray to cool completely (or use leftover cold rice for the best texture).
Dice the bacon, mince the garlic and slice the spring onion.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat and fry the bacon until golden and crisp, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the frozen mixed vegetables and stir-fry until heated through and tender-crisp, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry until heated through, about 3-4 minutes.
Pour in the kecap manis and sambal oelek and toss well to coat the rice evenly.
Meanwhile, fry the eggs in a separate pan to your liking, with crisp edges and a soft yolk.
Divide the nasi goreng between bowls, top each with a fried egg and spring onion, and serve.

Chicken Quesadillas.title: Chicken Quesadillas
method_steps: Dice the chicken breast.
Combine the cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and chilli powder in a small bowl.

Heat a little oil in a pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken with the spice mix, stirring occasionally, until cooked through (internal temperature of 75°C) and lightly golden, about 6-8 minutes.
Remove the chicken and shred with two forks.
Wipe out the pan, lay a tortilla in it and scatter over grated cheese and shredded chicken, then top with a second tortilla.
Toast over medium heat, pressing down gently, until golden and the cheese has melted, about 2-3 minutes each side.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas, chicken and cheese to make more quesadillas.
Slice into wedges and serve warm with coriander and sour cream.

Fried Rice with Egg & Peas.title: Fried Rice with Egg & Peas
method_steps: Cook the rice ahead of time and cool completely, or use leftover cold rice.
Finely dice the carrot, mince the garlic, slice the spring onion and whisk the eggs.
Heat a little oil in a wok over medium-high heat, pour in the egg and scramble until just set, then remove and set aside.
Add a little more oil to the wok and stir-fry the carrot and garlic until slightly softened, about 2 minutes.
Add the frozen peas and corn and stir-fry until heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry until piping hot, about 4-5 minutes.
Pour in the soy sauce and sesame oil and toss well to coat the rice evenly.
Fold the scrambled egg back through, scatter with spring onion, and serve immediately.

Beef Ramen Bowls.title: Beef Ramen Bowls
method_steps: Grate the ginger, mince the garlic and halve the bok choy.
Pour the beef stock into a saucepan, add the soy sauce, ginger and garlic, and bring to a simmer, cooking for 10 minutes to infuse.
Meanwhile, bring a separate pot of water to the boil and cook the ramen noodles according to the packet instructions, then drain.
Soft-boil the eggs in a small pot of simmering water for 6-7 minutes, then cool in cold water, peel and halve.
