Heat a little oil in a frying pan over high heat and sear the beef strips until browned but still tender, about 2-3 minutes, taking care not to overcook.
Add the bok choy to the broth for the last 2 minutes to wilt.
Divide the noodles between bowls and ladle over the hot broth and bok choy.
Top each bowl with the beef strips, egg half and spring onion, drizzle with sesame oil, and serve immediately.

Pesto Chicken Pasta.title: Pesto Chicken Pasta
method_steps: Slice the chicken breast and halve the cherry tomatoes.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the penne according to the packet instructions until al dente, then drain, reserving a little pasta water.
Meanwhile, heat a little oil in a pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken, turning occasionally, until golden and cooked through (internal temperature of 75°C), about 6-8 minutes.
Add the cherry tomatoes to the pan for the last 2 minutes and cook until just softened.
Add the drained pasta to the pan with the chicken and tomatoes.
Stir through the basil pesto, loosening with a splash of reserved pasta water if needed.
Toss through the parmesan and season to taste.
Serve immediately scattered with toasted pine nuts and extra parmesan.

Chicken Congee.title: Chicken Congee
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until it runs clear, then drain well.
Peel and finely chop the ginger, and thinly slice the spring onion, keeping the white and green parts separate.
Place the rice, chicken stock and ginger in a large heavy-based pot and bring to the boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer gently, stirring every few minutes so the rice doesn't stick, for about 30 minutes until thick and porridge-like.
Add the chicken thigh fillets to the pot and simmer for a further 8-10 minutes until cooked through and the chicken reaches 75°C.
Remove the chicken, shred it into bite-sized pieces with two forks, then stir it back through the congee.
Season with the soy sauce, adding a little extra stock if the congee is thicker than you'd like.
Ladle into bowls, scatter over the spring onion whites and greens, and serve with extra soy sauce on the side.

Tomato Soup with Grilled Cheese.title: Tomato Soup with Grilled Cheese
method_steps: Finely dice the onion.
Heat a little oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion for 4-5 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add the garlic and dried basil and cook for a further minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned tomatoes and vegetable stock, bring to a simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Blend the soup with a stick blender until smooth, then season with salt and pepper to taste.
Butter one side of each bread slice and sandwich the grated cheese between four slices, buttered sides facing out.
Cook the sandwiches in a frying pan or sandwich press over medium heat for 3-4 minutes each side until golden and the cheese has melted.
Slice the sandwiches in half and serve alongside bowls of hot tomato soup.

Beef Goulash.title: Beef Goulash
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into 3-4cm cubes, pat dry with paper towel and season with salt and pepper.
Finely chop the onion and slice the red capsicum into strips.
Heat oil in a large heavy-based pot or Dutch oven over high heat and brown the beef in batches, about 3-4 minutes per batch, until well coloured on all sides; remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook the onion and capsicum in the same pot for 5-6 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic, sweet paprika and caraway seeds and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato paste, then return the beef to the pot with the tinned tomatoes, beef stock and bay leaves, scraping up any browned bits from the base of the pot.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat for 1.5-2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is fork-tender.
About 15 minutes before serving, cook the pasta in a large pot of salted boiling water according to packet directions, then drain.
Remove the bay leaves and season the goulash with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the goulash over the pasta, topped with a dollop of sour cream.

Beef & Mushroom Pie.title: Beef & Mushroom Pie
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Cut the beef chuck into small 2cm cubes and season with salt and pepper.
Finely chop the onion and slice the mushrooms.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over high heat and brown the beef in batches, about 3 minutes per batch, until coloured; remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium, add the onion and garlic and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened, then add the mushrooms and cook for a further 3-4 minutes until golden.
Sprinkle over the plain flour and stir for 1 minute to cook out the raw flour taste.
Gradually stir in the beef stock and Worcestershire sauce, return the beef to the pan, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until thickened and the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Season to taste, then pour the filling into a pie dish and let it cool slightly.
Top with the puff pastry, trimming the edges and pressing to seal, and cut a small slit in the top to let steam escape.
Bake for 25 minutes until the pastry is puffed and golden, then rest for 5 minutes before serving.

Beef Kebabs with Rice.title: Beef Kebabs with Rice
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice, then cook in a saucepan of salted water according to packet directions until tender; drain and keep warm.
Cut the beef rump into 3cm cubes.
Cut the red capsicum, red onion and zucchini into chunks of a similar size to the beef.
Thread the beef and vegetables alternately onto skewers.
Combine the olive oil, crushed garlic, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, oregano and the juice of the lemon in a small bowl, then brush generously over the skewers.
Preheat a grill or barbecue to medium-high heat.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes, turning every couple of minutes, until the beef is cooked to your liking and the vegetables are lightly charred.
Rest the skewers for 2-3 minutes.
Serve the skewers over the warm rice.

Beef Pho.title: Beef Pho
method_steps: Char the onion and ginger under a hot grill or in a dry pan for 4-5 minutes until blackened in spots, then peel the ginger and slice.
Place the beef stock, star anise, cinnamon stick, charred onion and ginger in a large pot and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Simmer gently for 30 minutes to infuse the spices into the broth, then stir in the fish sauce and season with salt to taste.
Meanwhile, wrap the beef rump in plastic wrap and freeze for 15-20 minutes to firm it up, making it easier to slice thinly.
Cook the rice noodles according to packet directions, then drain and divide among four bowls.
Slice the beef as thinly as possible, against the grain.
Strain the hot broth to remove the aromatics, then ladle it over the raw beef and noodles — the heat of the broth will gently cook the beef.
Top with bean sprouts, coriander leaves and a squeeze of lime, then serve immediately.

Beef Rendang.title: Beef Rendang
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into 3-4cm cubes.
Bruise the lemongrass stalks with the back of a knife and tear the kaffir lime leaves to release their flavour.
Heat a little oil in a large heavy-based pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat and brown the beef in batches, about 3-4 minutes per batch, until well coloured.
Return all the beef to the pot, add the rendang paste and stir for 1-2 minutes until fragrant and the beef is well coated.
Pour in the coconut milk, add the lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves and brown sugar, and stir well, scraping up any browned bits from the base of the pot.

Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook uncovered for 2-2.5 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is very tender and the sauce has reduced to a dark, thick paste clinging to the meat.
Meanwhile, rinse the jasmine rice and cook in a saucepan of salted water according to packet directions until tender; drain and keep warm.
Remove the lemongrass and kaffir lime leaves, then taste the rendang and season with salt if needed.
Serve the rendang hot over the steamed rice.

Meatloaf with Vegetables.title: Meatloaf with Vegetables
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C and line a loaf tin with baking paper.
Finely grate the onion.

In a large bowl, combine the beef mince, breadcrumbs, eggs, grated onion, Worcestershire sauce and 1 tablespoon of the tomato sauce; season well with salt and pepper and mix with your hands until just combined, without overworking the mixture.
Press the mixture firmly into the prepared loaf tin and smooth the top.
Spread the remaining tomato sauce evenly over the top of the meatloaf.
Bake for 50 minutes, or until cooked through and the centre reaches 70°C.
Meanwhile, peel and chop the potatoes and boil in salted water for 15-20 minutes until tender; drain and mash with the butter and milk, seasoning to taste.
Steam or boil the green beans for 3-4 minutes until just tender and bright green.
Rest the meatloaf for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve the sliced meatloaf with the mash and green beans.

Beef Bourguignon.title: Beef Bourguignon
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into 3-4cm cubes, pat dry, and toss in the plain flour to lightly coat; cut the bacon into small strips.
Heat a little oil in a large heavy-based pot or Dutch oven over high heat and brown the beef in batches, about 3-4 minutes per batch, until well coloured; remove and set aside.
Add the bacon to the pot and cook for 3-4 minutes until golden and crisp; remove and set aside with the beef.
Finely chop the onion, slice the carrots, and halve or quarter the mushrooms.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook the onion and carrot in the pot for 5-6 minutes until softened, then add the mushrooms and cook for a further 3-4 minutes until golden.
Add the garlic and tomato paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the beef and bacon to the pot with the red wine, beef stock, thyme and bay leaves, stirring well and scraping up any browned bits from the base of the pot.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat for 2-2.5 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is very tender.
Remove the thyme and bay leaves, whisk in the butter, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot with creamy mash or crusty bread to mop up the sauce.

Salisbury Steak with Gravy.title: Salisbury Steak with Gravy
method_steps: Peel and chop the potatoes, place in a pot of cold salted water, bring to the boil and cook for 15-20 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, finely chop half the onion and combine with the beef mince, breadcrumbs, egg, 1 teaspoon of the Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper; mix well and shape into four oval patties.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat and brown the patties for 2-3 minutes each side until well coloured (they don't need to be fully cooked yet); remove and set aside.
Slice the remaining onion and the mushrooms, add to the pan and cook for 5 minutes until softened and golden.
Sprinkle over the plain flour and stir for 1 minute, then gradually whisk in the beef stock and remaining Worcestershire sauce until smooth.
Bring the gravy to a simmer, return the patties to the pan, and cook for 10 minutes until the patties are cooked through (internal temperature 70°C) and the gravy has thickened.
Drain the potatoes and mash with the butter and milk, seasoning to taste.
Serve the Salisbury steaks and gravy over the mash.

Beef Vindaloo.title: Beef Vindaloo
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into 3cm cubes.
Peel and chop the potatoes, and mince the garlic.
Heat oil in a large saucepan or pot over high heat and brown the beef in batches, about 3-4 minutes per batch, until coloured; remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium, add the garlic and vindaloo paste to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Return the beef to the pot, add the tinned tomatoes, beef stock, vinegar and potato, and stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat for 45-50 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beef and potato are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Meanwhile, rinse the basmati rice and cook in a saucepan of salted water according to packet directions until tender; drain and keep warm.
Season the vindaloo with salt to taste.
Serve hot over the steamed rice.

Steak Sandwiches.title: Steak Sandwiches
method_steps: Thinly slice the onions.
Heat a little oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the onions for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft and golden; remove and set aside.
Slice the tomato and cut the Turkish bread into four portions, splitting each in half.
Increase the heat to high, season the minute steaks with salt and pepper, and cook for 1-2 minutes each side until browned and cooked to your liking.
Rest the steaks for 2-3 minutes.
Toast the cut sides of the Turkish bread in the pan or under a grill until lightly golden.
Sprinkle the grated cheese over the warm bread bases so it melts slightly.
Layer the steak, caramelised onion, rocket and tomato over the cheese, then spread the tomato relish on the top half of the bread.
Sandwich together, cut in half and serve.

Beef Lasagne.title: Beef Lasagne
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C.
Finely chop the onion, garlic, carrot and celery.

Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat and cook the onion, carrot and celery for 5-6 minutes until softened, then add the garlic and beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, for 6-8 minutes until browned.
Stir in the tomato paste and passata, bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened; season with salt and pepper.
Meanwhile, make the cheese sauce: melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat, whisk in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute, then gradually whisk in the milk until smooth.
Simmer the cheese sauce, whisking often, for 4-5 minutes until thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon, then stir through half the grated cheese.
In a baking dish, layer the bolognese sauce, lasagne sheets and cheese sauce, repeating until all the ingredients are used, finishing with a layer of cheese sauce.
Sprinkle the remaining grated cheese over the top.
Bake for 40 minutes until the pasta is tender and the top is golden and bubbling.
Rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing and serving.

Beef & Barley Soup.title: Beef & Barley Soup
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into small 2cm cubes.
Finely dice the onion, carrot and celery.
Heat oil in a large pot over high heat and brown the beef in batches, about 3-4 minutes per batch, until coloured; remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium, add the onion, carrot and celery to the pot and cook for 5-6 minutes until softened.
Rinse the pearl barley, then add it to the pot along with the beef stock, tinned tomatoes, bay leaf, thyme and the browned beef, stirring to combine.
Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat and simmer, partially covered, for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beef is tender and the barley is cooked through.
Remove the bay leaf and season with salt and pepper to taste, adding a splash more stock if the soup is thicker than you'd like.
Ladle into bowls and serve hot.

Chilli Beef Noodles.title: Chilli Beef Noodles
method_steps: Cook the egg noodles in a pot of boiling water according to packet directions, then drain and set aside.
Quarter the bok choy lengthways, and mince the garlic and ginger.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until just smoking.
Add the garlic, ginger and beef mince and stir-fry, breaking up the mince with a spoon, for 5-6 minutes until well browned and starting to crisp at the edges.
Add the bok choy and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until just wilted.
Stir in the chilli-garlic sauce and soy sauce and toss to coat.
Add the drained noodles and sesame oil to the wok and toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Chicken Chow Mein.title: Chicken Chow Mein
method_steps: Cook the egg noodles in a pot of boiling water according to packet directions, then drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the chicken breast, shred the cabbage, julienne the carrot, and mince the garlic and ginger.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until just smoking.
Add the chicken, garlic and ginger and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through, then remove and set aside.
Add a little more oil, then the cabbage and carrot, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until just softened.
Return the chicken to the wok along with the noodles, soy sauce and oyster sauce.
Toss everything together with the sesame oil over high heat for 1-2 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Chicken Biryani.title: Chicken Biryani
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water, then parboil in a large pot of salted water for 6-7 minutes until just tender but not fully cooked; drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the onions.
Cut the chicken thigh fillets into large chunks and toss with the biryani masala and half the yoghurt; set aside to marinate briefly.
Heat the ghee in a large heavy-based pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat and cook the onions for 6-8 minutes until softened and golden.
Add the bay leaf, cardamom pods, cinnamon stick and cloves and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the marinated chicken to the pot and brown for 5-6 minutes until coloured on all sides.
Spread the chicken mixture evenly over the base of the pot, then layer the parboiled rice on top, dollop over the remaining yoghurt and scatter with mint and coriander.
Cover tightly with a lid (foil first, if needed) and cook over very low heat for 20 minutes, allowing the rice to steam through without lifting the lid.
Remove from the heat and rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the rice gently with a fork to combine with the chicken and serve.

Chicken Katsu Curry.title: Chicken Katsu Curry
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice and cook in a saucepan of salted water according to packet directions until tender; drain and keep warm.
Slice the chicken breast in half horizontally to make thinner fillets, then season with salt and pepper.
Set up three shallow dishes: plain flour in one, beaten eggs in another, and panko breadcrumbs in the third; coat each chicken fillet in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs.

Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and fry the crumbed chicken for 3-4 minutes each side until golden, crisp and cooked through (internal temperature 75°C); drain on paper towel, then slice.
Meanwhile, finely chop the onion and grate the carrot, then cook in a little of the oil over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until softened.

Stir in the curry powder and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant, then add a splash of water, the soy sauce and honey, and simmer for 8-10 minutes until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the sliced katsu chicken over the rice, generously spooned with the curry sauce.

Chicken Marsala.title: Chicken Marsala
method_steps: Cook the fettuccine in a large pot of salted boiling water according to packet directions until al dente, then drain.
Slice the chicken breast horizontally into thin fillets, season with salt and pepper, and dust lightly in the plain flour.
Heat the butter and a little oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 3-4 minutes each side until golden; remove and set aside.
Slice the mushrooms, add to the same pan and cook for 4-5 minutes until golden and softened.
Pour in the marsala wine, scraping up any browned bits from the base of the pan, and simmer for 1-2 minutes to reduce slightly.
Add the chicken stock and simmer for 2 minutes.
Stir in the thickened cream and simmer for 3-4 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and coats the back of a spoon.
Return the chicken to the pan and simmer for a further 5 minutes until heated through and the chicken reaches 75°C.
Season to taste and serve the chicken and sauce over the fettuccine.

Chicken & Mushroom Risotto.title: Chicken & Mushroom Risotto
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces and slice the mushrooms.
Finely chop the onion and bring the chicken stock to a gentle simmer in a saucepan, keeping it warm over low heat.
Heat half the butter in a large, wide saucepan or frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through; remove and set aside.
Add a little more butter to the pan, then the mushrooms, and cook for 4-5 minutes until golden; remove and set aside with the chicken.
Add the onion to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened, then add the arborio rice and stir for 1-2 minutes until the grains are glossy and lightly toasted.
Pour in the white wine and stir until absorbed.

Add the warm stock a ladleful at a time, stirring frequently and letting each ladleful absorb before adding the next, for about 18-20 minutes until the rice is tender but still has a slight bite and the risotto is creamy.
Stir the chicken and mushrooms back through the risotto, then remove from the heat and stir in the remaining butter and the grated parmesan.
Season to taste and serve immediately while creamy and hot.

Chicken Kiev with Vegetables.title: Chicken Kiev with Vegetables
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray.
Mash the softened butter with crushed garlic, chopped parsley and a pinch of salt to make garlic butter.
Cut a deep pocket into each chicken breast and pack in two-thirds of the garlic butter, pressing the edges closed.
Dip each stuffed breast in beaten egg, then coat evenly in breadcrumbs.
Bake for 25 minutes until golden and the chicken reaches 75°C in the centre.
Meanwhile, boil the potato until tender, about 15 minutes, then drain and mash with the milk and remaining butter.
Steam or microwave the peas until just tender.
Rest the kiev for 2 minutes before serving with the mash and peas.

Chicken Shawarma Wraps.title: Chicken Shawarma Wraps
method_steps: Combine the cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, garlic powder, cinnamon, pepper and cayenne in a bowl.
Toss the chicken thigh fillet with the spice mix, olive oil and a squeeze of lemon juice until well coated.
Heat a chargrill pan or barbecue plate over medium-high heat.
Cook the chicken for 6-8 minutes, turning once, until well charred and cooked through (75°C).
Rest the chicken for 3 minutes, then slice into strips.
Warm the flatbread in a dry pan or oven for a minute each side.
Layer the flatbread with salad leaves, tomato, cucumber and red onion.
Top with the sliced chicken, drizzle with garlic sauce, and roll up firmly to serve.

Chicken Souvlaki Plate.title: Chicken Souvlaki Plate
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillet into even cubes.
Combine the chicken with the lemon juice, oregano, crushed garlic and olive oil in a bowl; marinate for at least 10 minutes.
Thread the chicken onto skewers.
Heat a barbecue or grill pan to medium-high and cook the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning regularly, until charred and cooked through (75°C).
Meanwhile, dice the tomato, cucumber and red onion, and toss with the olives and feta for a Greek salad.
Rest the skewers for a few minutes off the heat.
Serve the souvlaki with the Greek salad and a generous dollop of tzatziki.

Chicken Pot Pie.title: Chicken Pot Pie
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Dice the onion, carrot and celery, and cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces.
Melt the butter in a large pan and cook the onion, carrot and celery over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the chicken and cook, stirring, until browned on all sides.
Sprinkle in the flour and thyme, and stir for 1 minute to cook out the raw taste.
Gradually pour in the chicken stock, stirring constantly, and simmer until the sauce thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Stir in the cream and peas, then season to taste.
Pour the filling into a pie dish and top with the puff pastry, trimming and pressing the edges to seal.
Bake for 25 minutes until the pastry is puffed and deep golden.

Sticky Chicken Wings with Rice.title: Sticky Chicken Wings with Rice
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Toss the chicken wings with the soy sauce, honey, garlic and ginger until well coated.
Arrange the wings in a single layer on a lined tray.
Bake for 35 minutes, turning and basting with the pan juices halfway through, until sticky, dark golden and cooked through (75°C).
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Steam the bok choy for 2-3 minutes until just wilted.
Sprinkle the wings with sesame seeds.
Serve the wings hot over rice with the steamed greens alongside.

Chicken Adobo.title: Chicken Adobo
method_steps: Place the chicken thigh cutlets in a large pot with the soy sauce, vinegar, crushed garlic, bay leaves and peppercorns.
Bring to the boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook, covered, for 40 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
Stir the brown sugar into the sauce, then increase the heat and simmer, uncovered, for 5-8 minutes until reduced and glossy.
Return the chicken to the pan and turn to coat in the reduced sauce.
Serve the adobo over the rice, spooning over extra sauce.

Chicken Tortilla Soup.title: Chicken Tortilla Soup
method_steps: Dice the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion and garlic for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the cumin and chilli powder and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chicken breast, diced tomatoes, corn and chicken stock, then bring to a simmer.
Simmer gently for 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked through (75°C).
Remove the chicken, shred with two forks, then return it to the pot.
Season the soup to taste and squeeze in the lime juice.
Ladle into bowls and top with crushed corn chips, diced avocado and coriander.
Serve with the remaining lime cut into wedges.

Chicken & Sweetcorn Soup.title: Chicken & Sweetcorn Soup
method_steps: Bring the chicken stock to a gentle boil in a large pot.
Add the chicken breast and poach for 10-12 minutes until cooked through (75°C).
Remove the chicken, shred finely with two forks, then return it to the pot.
Stir in the creamed corn and bring back to a simmer.
Mix the cornflour with a little cold water to a smooth paste, then stir into the soup until it thickens slightly.
Beat the eggs in a jug, then drizzle slowly into the simmering soup while stirring gently to form ribbons.
Stir through the sesame oil and white pepper, and season to taste.
Ladle into bowls and scatter with sliced spring onion.

Coq au Vin.title: Coq au Vin
method_steps: Dice the bacon, slice the onion and mushrooms, and crush the garlic.
Heat a large heavy-based pot over medium-high heat and cook the bacon until golden, then remove and set aside.
Brown the chicken thigh cutlets in the bacon fat, in batches, for 3-4 minutes per side, then remove.
Add the onion, mushrooms and garlic to the pot and cook for 5 minutes until softened and lightly golden.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, then pour in the red wine and simmer for 2 minutes to cook off the alcohol.
Return the chicken and bacon to the pot, add the chicken stock, thyme sprigs and bay leaf, and bring to a simmer.
Cover and simmer gently for 1-1.5 hours, until the chicken is very tender.
Mash the butter and flour together to a paste, then whisk into the sauce and simmer a few minutes until thickened.
Remove the thyme stems and bay leaf, season to taste, and serve hot with mash.

Chicken Burgers.title: Chicken Burgers
method_steps: Preheat the oven and cook the oven chips according to packet instructions.
Flatten the chicken breast into even patties using a rolling pin or the flat of your hand.
Coat each patty in flour, then dip in beaten egg, then coat evenly in breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat and fry the patties for 4-5 minutes each side until golden and cooked through (75°C).
Rest the patties briefly on paper towel to drain.
Toast the burger buns lightly, cut-side down, in a dry pan.
Shred the lettuce and slice the tomato.
Build the burgers with the crumbed patty, lettuce, tomato and a spoonful of mayonnaise.
Serve immediately with the oven chips.

Chicken Meatball Subs.title: Chicken Meatball Subs
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Mix the chicken mince with the breadcrumbs, egg, crushed garlic, oregano, parmesan and a pinch of salt and pepper, then roll into even balls.
Place on a lined tray and bake for 15 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Meanwhile, warm the tomato passata in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the baked meatballs to the sauce and simmer gently for 5 minutes to coat and heat through.
Split the sub rolls and fill each with meatballs and plenty of sauce.
Scatter the grated cheese generously over the top.
Grill until the cheese is melted and bubbling, then serve hot.

Chicken Fajita Bowls.title: Chicken Fajita Bowls
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Combine the cumin, chilli powder, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and oregano in a small bowl.
Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and toss with the spice mix.
Heat oil in a large pan or wok over high heat and sear the chicken for 4-5 minutes until browned and cooked through, then remove.
Slice the capsicum and onion, add to the pan and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened and lightly charred.
Return the chicken to the pan and toss through to combine.
Warm the black beans in a small saucepan.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the chicken and vegetable mixture, black beans, salsa, diced avocado and a squeeze of lime.

Lemon Chicken (Chinese-Style).title: Lemon Chicken (Chinese-Style)
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Slice the chicken breast into thin strips.
Dip the chicken in beaten egg, then coat in cornflour, shaking off the excess.
Heat oil in a wok or large pan over medium-high heat and fry the chicken in batches for 3-4 minutes until golden and crisp, then drain on paper towel.
Squeeze the lemon juice into a small saucepan, add the sugar, chicken stock and remaining cornflour mixed with a splash of water, and simmer for 2-3 minutes until glossy and thickened.
Toss the fried chicken through the lemon sauce until well coated.
Serve immediately over the rice, scattered with sesame seeds.

Pork Ramen.title: Pork Ramen
method_steps: Heat a pan over medium-high heat and sear the pork loin for 3-4 minutes per side until browned and cooked through (65-70°C internal).
Rest the pork for 5 minutes, then slice thinly.
Bring the chicken stock to a simmer in a large pot, then stir in the miso paste, ginger, garlic, soy sauce and sesame oil until dissolved.
Boil the eggs for 6-7 minutes for a soft-boiled centre, then cool in cold water, peel and halve.
Cook the ramen noodles according to packet instructions, then drain.
Add the bok choy to the simmering broth for the last minute, just until wilted.
Divide the noodles among bowls and ladle over the hot broth and bok choy.
Top each bowl with sliced pork, a soft-boiled egg half and sliced spring onion, then serve immediately.

Char Siu Pork with Rice.title: Char Siu Pork with Rice
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Combine the char siu sauce and five-spice, then marinate the pork scotch fillet in the mixture for at least 10 minutes, reserving some for basting.
Place the pork on a lined tray and roast for 35 minutes, basting with the reserved sauce every 10 minutes, until caramelised and cooked through (70°C).
Brush with the honey for the final 5 minutes of roasting to glaze.
Rest the pork for 5 minutes before slicing.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Steam the bok choy for 2-3 minutes until just tender.
Slice the pork and serve over the rice with the bok choy, drizzled with any remaining pan juices.

Pork Katsu with Rice.title: Pork Katsu with Rice
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Finely shred the cabbage and set aside.
Coat each pork loin steak in flour, then beaten egg, then press into the panko breadcrumbs to coat evenly.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat and fry the pork for 3-4 minutes each side until deep golden and cooked through (70°C).
Rest the pork for 2 minutes, then slice into strips.
Divide the rice among plates and top with the sliced pork and shredded cabbage.
Drizzle generously with tonkatsu sauce and serve.

Sweet Chilli Pork Stir Fry.title: Sweet Chilli Pork Stir Fry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Heat a wok or large pan over high heat until smoking, then stir-fry the pork strips for 2-3 minutes until browned, and remove.
Slice the capsicum, trim the snow peas, and crush the garlic and ginger.
Add the garlic, ginger and vegetables to the wok and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until crisp-tender.
Return the pork to the wok.
Add the sweet chilli sauce and soy sauce, and toss everything together until well coated and glossy.
Serve immediately over the rice.

Pork Larb with Rice.title: Pork Larb with Rice
method_steps: Dry-toast the jasmine rice in a small pan over medium heat until golden and fragrant, then grind to a coarse powder.
Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Heat a dry pan or a little oil over medium-high heat and fry the pork mince, breaking it up with a spoon, for 6-8 minutes until well browned and cooked through.
Remove from the heat, let cool slightly, then stir in the toasted rice powder.
Squeeze in the lime juice and stir through the fish sauce.
Thinly slice the red onion and chilli, roughly chop the mint and coriander leaves, then toss through the pork.
Separate the iceberg lettuce into cups.
Serve the larb spooned into lettuce cups, alongside the steamed rice.

Pork & Fennel Sausage Pasta.title: Pork & Fennel Sausage Pasta
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the rigatoni until al dente, then reserve a cup of the cooking water and drain.
Meanwhile, dice the onion and crush the garlic.
Squeeze the sausage meat out of its casings into a hot, lightly oiled pan.
Brown the sausage meat over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon, for 5-6 minutes.
Add the onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened, then add the garlic, fennel seeds and chilli flakes and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tomato passata and simmer for 8-10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Toss the drained pasta through the sauce, loosening with a splash of the reserved pasta water if needed.
Serve topped with grated parmesan.

Bacon & Egg Pie.title: Bacon & Egg Pie
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Line a pie dish with the shortcrust pastry, pressing it into the base and sides.
Dice the bacon and fry in a pan over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until golden, then scatter over the pastry base.
Slice the spring onion and scatter over the bacon, along with the grated cheese.
Whisk the eggs with the milk and season with pepper, then pour over the filling.
Top with the puff pastry, pressing the edges to seal and trimming any excess.
Cut a few small slits in the top to let steam escape.
Bake for 35 minutes until the pastry is golden and the egg is set.
Rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.

Pork Carnitas Tacos.title: Pork Carnitas Tacos
method_steps: Zest and juice the orange; peel and roughly chop the onion and crush the garlic.
Trim the pork shoulder of excess fat and cut into large chunks.
Place the pork in a slow cooker or heavy-based pot with the orange juice and zest, onion, garlic, cumin, oregano and bay leaves, and season generously with salt and pepper.
Add enough water to just cover, cover and cook on low for about 2 hours, until the pork is fork-tender.
Remove the pork, discard the bay leaves, and shred with two forks.
Heat a large frying pan over high heat, add the shredded pork with a little of the cooking liquid, and fry for 5-8 minutes until the edges turn golden and crisp.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or microwave.
Fill the tortillas with the crispy pork, top with salsa and coriander.
Squeeze over lime juice and serve immediately.

Bangers & Colcannon.title: Bangers & Colcannon
method_steps: Peel and chop the potatoes into even chunks and place in a pot of cold, salted water.
Bring to the boil and cook for 12-15 minutes until tender when pierced with a knife.
Meanwhile, finely shred the cabbage and slice the spring onions.
Heat a frying pan over medium heat and cook the sausages for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned all over and cooked through with no pink in the centre.
Add the shredded cabbage to the potato pot for the final 4-5 minutes of cooking, until softened.
Drain the potato and cabbage well and return to the pot; warm the milk with the spring onions in a small saucepan for 2 minutes until softened.
Mash the potato and cabbage with the butter and warm milk mixture until smooth, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Rest the sausages for a couple of minutes, then slice on an angle.
Serve the sausages over a generous mound of colcannon.

Lamb Rogan Josh.title: Lamb Rogan Josh
method_steps: Trim the lamb shoulder of excess fat and cut into 3cm chunks; finely dice the onion, crush the garlic and grate the ginger.
Heat a large heavy-based pot over high heat with a little oil, and brown the lamb in batches for 4-5 minutes per batch, until deeply coloured on all sides, then set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium, add the onion and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened; add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the rogan josh paste and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Return the lamb to the pot with the diced tomatoes and lamb stock, and stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer, cover and cook on low for about 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is tender.
Stir through the yoghurt and simmer uncovered for a further 5 minutes until the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Season the curry to taste and serve hot over the rice.

Lamb Tagine.title: Lamb Tagine
method_steps: Trim the lamb shoulder of excess fat and cut into 3cm chunks; finely dice the onion and carrot, and crush the garlic.
Heat a heavy-based pot or tagine over medium-high heat with a little oil, and brown the lamb in batches for 4-5 minutes per batch until well coloured on all sides, then set aside.
Add the onion and carrot to the pot and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened; add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, ginger, turmeric, paprika and cayenne, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the lamb to the pot with the dried apricots, drained chickpeas, honey and enough water to just cover.
Bring to a simmer, cover and cook on low for about 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is fork-tender.
Meanwhile, prepare the couscous according to the packet instructions and fluff with a fork.
Toast the almonds in a dry pan for 2 minutes until golden.
Season the tagine to taste and serve spooned over the couscous, scattered with almonds and coriander.

Slow-Braised Lamb Shanks.title: Slow-Braised Lamb Shanks
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 160°C.
Finely dice the onion, carrot and celery, and crush the garlic.
Heat a large ovenproof pot over high heat with a little oil, and brown the lamb shanks well on all sides, about 8-10 minutes total, then remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium, add the onion, carrot, celery and garlic, and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened; stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the red wine and simmer for 2 minutes to reduce slightly; return the shanks to the pot with the stock, diced tomatoes, rosemary, thyme and bay leaves.
Bring to a simmer, cover and braise in the oven for 2.5-3 hours, turning once, until the meat is falling off the bone.
Meanwhile, boil the potatoes in salted water for 12-15 minutes until tender; drain and mash with the butter and milk until smooth.
Remove the herb sprigs and bay leaves from the sauce and skim any excess fat.
Rest the shanks for a few minutes, then serve over the mash with the sauce spooned over.

Lamb Cutlets with Couscous.title: Lamb Cutlets with Couscous
method_steps: Preheat a grill or char-grill pan to high heat.
Crush the garlic, zest and juice the lemon, and slice the zucchini and capsicum into bite-sized pieces.
Toss the vegetables in a little of the olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the vegetables for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until charred and tender.
Meanwhile, prepare the couscous according to the packet instructions and fluff with a fork.
Rub the lamb cutlets with the garlic, lemon zest, oregano and remaining olive oil, and season well.
Grill the cutlets for about 3 minutes each side for medium, until well-browned and cooked to your liking.
Rest the cutlets for 3-4 minutes.
Toss the couscous with the roasted vegetables and lemon juice, and serve alongside the rested cutlets.

Greek Lamb Traybake.title: Greek Lamb Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large roasting tray.
Cut the lamb leg into large chunks, chop the potato, capsicum and onion into similar-sized pieces, and crush the garlic.
Zest and juice the lemon.
Toss the lamb and vegetables in a large bowl with the lemon juice and zest, garlic, dried oregano, olive oil, salt and pepper.
Spread everything out in a single, even layer on the prepared tray.
Roast for 45 minutes, turning halfway through, until the lamb is cooked through and the vegetables are golden and tender.
Scatter the olives and crumbled feta over the tray for the final 5 minutes of cooking.
Rest for 5 minutes.
Serve the traybake with a generous dollop of tzatziki.

Lamb Meatballs in Tomato Sauce.title: Lamb Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
method_steps: Combine the lamb mince, grated onion, garlic, breadcrumbs, egg, cumin and chopped parsley in a large bowl, and season with salt and pepper.
Roll the mixture into 16-20 small meatballs, about the size of a golf ball.
Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat with a little oil.
Brown the meatballs for 4-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until coloured all over (they don't need to be cooked through yet).
Pour in the tomato passata and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover and simmer for 15 minutes, until the meatballs are cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Meanwhile, prepare the couscous according to the packet instructions.
Season the sauce to taste and serve the meatballs over the couscous.

Lamb Burgers.title: Lamb Burgers
method_steps: Combine the lamb mince with the crushed garlic, cumin and chopped mint in a bowl, and season with salt and pepper.
Shape into 4 patties, pressing a slight dimple into the centre of each.
Preheat a barbecue or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill the patties for 5-6 minutes each side, until well-browned and cooked through.
Rest the patties for 2-3 minutes.
Lightly toast the burger buns, cut-side down, on the grill, and slice the tomato.
Spread tzatziki over the base of each bun.
Top each with a patty, tomato slices, salad leaves and more tzatziki, then sandwich with the top of the bun.
Serve the burgers immediately.

Lamb Saag.title: Lamb Saag
method_steps: Trim the lamb shoulder and cut into 2-3cm chunks; finely dice the onion, crush the garlic and grate the ginger.
Heat a large heavy-based pot over medium-high heat with a little oil, and brown the lamb in batches for 4-5 minutes per batch until coloured, then set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened; add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the turmeric and garam masala and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the lamb to the pot with a splash of water, cover and simmer on low for about 40 minutes until tender.
Add the frozen spinach and cook for a further 5 minutes until wilted and heated through.
Stir through the cream and simmer uncovered for 5 minutes until the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Season to taste and serve the saag over the rice.

Salmon Fishcakes.title: Salmon Fishcakes
method_steps: Peel and chop the potatoes, then boil in salted water for 12-15 minutes until tender; drain, mash and set aside to cool slightly.
Meanwhile, pan-fry or poach the salmon fillets for 4-5 minutes until just cooked through, then flake into large pieces, removing any skin and bones.
Finely slice the spring onion and chop the dill.
Combine the mashed potato, flaked salmon, spring onion and dill in a large bowl and season with salt and pepper.
Beat the egg and mix half through the salmon mixture to bind, reserving the rest for crumbing.
Shape the mixture into 8 even patties.
Dip each patty in the remaining beaten egg, then coat in the breadcrumbs, pressing to adhere.
Heat a little oil in a frying pan over medium heat and pan-fry the cakes for 3-4 minutes each side, until golden and heated through.
Serve the fishcakes with the salad leaves and lemon wedges.

Prawn Laksa.title: Prawn Laksa
method_steps: Soak the rice noodles in hot water according to the packet instructions until softened, then drain.
Peel and devein the prawns if needed.
Heat a large pot over medium heat with a little oil and fry the laksa paste for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk, chicken stock and fish sauce, stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Simmer for 5-10 minutes to let the flavours develop.
Add the prawns and cook for 3 minutes until pink and just cooked through.
Add the noodles and bean sprouts, and warm through for 1-2 minutes.
Season to taste and ladle into bowls.
Top with coriander and serve with lime wedges.

Prawn Curry.title: Prawn Curry
method_steps: Peel and devein the prawns if needed; finely dice the onion and crush the garlic.
Heat a large pot or wok over medium heat with a little oil, and cook the onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and curry paste, and fry for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk and diced tomatoes, stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the prawns and cook for 4 minutes until pink and just cooked through, taking care not to overcook them.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Season the curry to taste, scatter with coriander, and serve over the rice.

Beer-Battered Fish with Chips.title: Beer-Battered Fish with Chips
method_steps: Preheat the oven according to the oven chips packet instructions.
Spread the oven chips on a baking tray in a single layer and bake according to the packet instructions, about 20-25 minutes, until golden and crisp.
Meanwhile, whisk the self-raising flour with the beer in a bowl until you have a smooth, thick batter that coats the back of a spoon.
Pat the fish fillets dry with paper towel and season with salt.
Heat a generous amount of oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Dip each fillet in the batter, letting the excess drip off, then carefully lower into the hot oil.
Shallow-fry the fish for 3-4 minutes each side, until the batter is golden and crisp and the fish flakes easily.
Drain the fish on paper towel, and cook the peas in a small pot of boiling water for 2-3 minutes until tender.
Serve the fish and chips with the peas and lemon wedges.

Prawn Tacos.title: Prawn Tacos
method_steps: Peel and devein the prawns if needed, and pat dry with paper towel.
Combine the cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder and chilli powder in a small bowl.
Toss the prawns with the spice mix until evenly coated.
Finely shred the cabbage and slice the avocado, and roughly chop the coriander.
Heat a little oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
Pan-fry the prawns for 2-3 minutes each side, until pink, opaque and just cooked through.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or microwave.
Fill the tortillas with the prawns, shredded cabbage, avocado and coriander.
Squeeze over lime juice and serve immediately.

Seafood Marinara Pasta.title: Seafood Marinara Pasta
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the spaghetti according to the packet instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, finely chop the garlic and roughly chop the parsley.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the garlic and chilli flakes for 1 minute until fragrant, taking care not to let it brown.
Add the marinara seafood mix and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring gently.
Pour in the white wine and simmer for 1-2 minutes to reduce.
Add the tomato passata and simmer for 5 minutes until the seafood is just cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Drain the pasta, reserving a little of the cooking water.
Toss the pasta through the sauce, adding a splash of the reserved water if needed to loosen.
Stir through the parsley and serve immediately.

Kedgeree.title: Kedgeree
method_steps: Place the eggs in a pot of cold water, bring to the boil and cook for 8-9 minutes for firm yolks, then drain, cool under cold water, peel and quarter.
Rinse the basmati rice; melt the butter in a pot, stir in the curry powder for 30 seconds, add the rice and cook according to the packet instructions until tender.
Meanwhile, poach or steam the smoked fish fillets for 6-8 minutes until cooked through and flaking easily, then flake into large pieces, removing skin and bones.
Cook the frozen peas in a small pot of boiling water for 2-3 minutes until tender, then drain.
Fold the flaked fish and peas through the cooked rice.
Season to taste with salt and pepper, and chop the parsley.
Top with the quartered boiled eggs and parsley.
Serve warm with lemon wedges.

Baked Whole Snapper.title: Baked Whole Snapper
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large baking tray.
Pat the snapper dry inside and out with paper towel, and score the skin a few times on each side.
Slice one lemon, thinly slice the garlic and roughly chop the parsley.
Stuff the cavity of the snapper with the lemon slices, garlic and parsley.
Drizzle the fish with the olive oil and season generously with salt and pepper, inside and out.
Bake for 25 minutes, until the flesh is opaque and flakes easily from the bone.
Meanwhile, steam or blanch the broccolini for 3-4 minutes until bright green and just tender.
Rest the fish for a few minutes, then juice the remaining lemon over the top.
Serve the snapper with the steamed broccolini and a side salad.

Prawn Pad Thai.title: Prawn Pad Thai
method_steps: Soak the rice noodles in hot water according to the packet instructions until softened, then drain.
Peel and devein the prawns if needed, lightly beat the eggs, crush the garlic, and roughly chop the peanuts and garlic chives.
Heat a little oil in a large wok over high heat and stir-fry the garlic and prawns for 2 minutes until the prawns are starting to turn pink.
Push the prawns to one side of the wok, pour the egg into the space and scramble for 1 minute until just set.
Add the drained noodles and pad thai sauce, tossing everything together for 2-3 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Add the bean sprouts and garlic chives and toss for a further minute until just wilted.
Divide among bowls and top with the chopped peanuts.
Serve with lime wedges to squeeze over.

Thai Fish Cakes with Rice.title: Thai Fish Cakes with Rice
method_steps: Finely slice the green beans and kaffir lime leaves.
Blend or finely chop the white fish fillets with the red curry paste, egg and fish sauce in a food processor until a smooth, sticky paste forms.
Transfer to a bowl and mix through the sliced green beans and kaffir lime leaves.
Shape the mixture into 8 small, flat patties, wetting your hands to prevent sticking.
Heat a little oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Fry the fish cakes for 3-4 minutes each side, until golden and cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Serve the fish cakes with the rice and sweet chilli sauce for dipping.

Smoked Salmon Pasta.title: Smoked Salmon Pasta
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the fettuccine until al dente, about 10-12 minutes.
Meanwhile, zest and juice the lemon and roughly chop the dill.
Pour the thickened cream into a large frying pan over medium heat, add the frozen peas and simmer for 3-4 minutes until the peas are tender.
Stir in the lemon zest and juice.
Reduce the heat to low and fold in the smoked salmon, warming gently for 1-2 minutes without letting it boil so it stays silky.
Reserve a cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the pasta.
Toss the drained pasta through the sauce with the dill, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if it needs loosening.
Season with black pepper, scatter with parmesan and serve immediately.

Garlic Butter Prawns with Rice.title: Garlic Butter Prawns with Rice
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice, then cook according to packet directions and keep warm.
Peel the prawns if needed, mince the garlic, chop the parsley, and zest and juice the lemon.
Melt the butter in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds, taking care not to let it brown.
Add the prawns in a single layer and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until pink, opaque and curled.
Squeeze in the lemon juice and toss to coat the prawns in the garlic butter.
Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon the rice into bowls, top with the prawns and pan juices, and finish with parsley and lemon zest.

Tuna Pasta Salad.title: Tuna Pasta Salad
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the pasta spirals until al dente, about 10-12 minutes.
Drain the pasta, rinse under cold water to stop the cooking and cool it down, then drain well again.
Meanwhile, drain the tuna and the corn.
Halve the cherry tomatoes and finely dice the red onion and celery.
Combine the cooled pasta, tuna, corn, cherry tomatoes, red onion and celery in a large bowl.
Add the mayonnaise and a squeeze of lemon juice, and toss gently until everything is well coated.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve straight away, or chill for 20 minutes for a cooler salad.

Chana Masala.title: Chana Masala
method_steps: Cook the rice according to packet directions and keep warm.
Dice the onion and mince the garlic and ginger.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat, add the cumin seeds and cook for 30 seconds until sizzling, then add the onion and cook until soft and golden, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the ground coriander, turmeric, chilli powder and garam masala and cook for 30 seconds to toast the spices.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas, then add them to the pan with the tinned tomatoes.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and coats the back of a spoon, lightly mashing a few chickpeas for a thicker texture if you like.
Season with salt and squeeze over a little lemon juice.
Serve hot over the rice, scattered with fresh coriander.

Palak Paneer.title: Palak Paneer
method_steps: Cook the rice according to packet directions and keep warm.
Wilt the frozen spinach in a saucepan over medium heat until soft, then blend or finely chop until smooth.
Cut the paneer into cubes.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat and fry the paneer until golden on all sides, about 3-4 minutes, then remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add the onion, garlic, ginger, cumin seeds and green chilli, and cook until the onion is softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the garam masala and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the spinach and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the cream and simmer for 2 minutes until the sauce is glossy and coats the back of a spoon.
Fold the paneer back through and warm for 2 minutes, then season with salt and serve with the rice.

Vegetable Biryani.title: Vegetable Biryani
method_steps: Rinse the rice, then boil in salted water for 5-6 minutes until just partly tender; drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the onions.
Heat oil in a large heavy-based pot and fry the onions over medium heat until deeply golden, about 8 minutes; remove half for garnish.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the biryani spice and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the frozen mixed vegetables and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Spoon the yoghurt evenly over the vegetables.
Layer the par-cooked rice on top without stirring it through, then scatter over the cashews, mint and coriander.
Cover tightly with a lid and steam over low heat for 20 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and fragrant.
Fluff gently with a fork to combine the layers, top with the reserved fried onions and serve.

Tofu Pad Thai.title: Tofu Pad Thai
method_steps: Soak the rice noodles in hot water according to packet directions until pliable, then drain.
Pat the tofu dry with paper towel and cut into cubes; mince the garlic.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat, add the tofu and fry until golden and crisp on the edges, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Push the tofu to one side of the wok, crack in the eggs and scramble until just set, about 1-2 minutes, then mix through the tofu.
Add the drained noodles, pad thai sauce and chilli flakes, tossing everything together over high heat until coated and heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
Add most of the peanuts and bean sprouts and toss briefly to combine, about 1 minute.
Divide among bowls and top with the remaining peanuts, bean sprouts and lime wedges.
Serve immediately while hot.

Eggplant Parmigiana.title: Eggplant Parmigiana
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C.
Slice the eggplant into 1cm rounds, salt lightly and set aside for 10 minutes to draw out moisture, then pat dry with paper towel.
Set up a dredging station with the plain flour and beaten egg, then coat each eggplant slice in flour, then egg.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat and fry the eggplant in batches until golden on both sides, about 3 minutes per side; drain on paper towel.
Warm the tomato passata with the crushed garlic in a small saucepan for 2-3 minutes.
Spoon a little garlic passata over the base of a baking dish, then layer the fried eggplant, more passata and a scattering of mozzarella, repeating until everything is used.
Finish the top with the remaining passata and a generous layer of parmesan.
Bake at 190°C for 30 minutes until bubbling and golden on top.
Rest for 5 minutes, scatter with fresh basil, and serve with salad or crusty bread.

Aloo Gobi.title: Aloo Gobi
method_steps: Cook the rice according to packet directions and keep warm.
Peel and dice the potatoes into 2cm chunks, cut the cauliflower into florets, and dice the onion, garlic and ginger.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat, add the cumin seeds and cook for 30 seconds until sizzling.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the turmeric and curry powder and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the potato and cauliflower, stirring to coat well in the spiced onion.
Add a splash of water, cover, and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt, scatter with fresh coriander, and serve hot with rice or naan.

Spinach & Ricotta Cannelloni.title: Spinach & Ricotta Cannelloni
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C.
Thaw the frozen spinach and squeeze out as much liquid as possible, then chop finely.
In a bowl, mix the ricotta, spinach, egg, parmesan and nutmeg together and season with salt and pepper.
Spoon or pipe the ricotta mixture into the cannelloni tubes.
Warm the tomato passata with the crushed garlic in a saucepan for 2-3 minutes, then spread a little over the base of a baking dish.
Arrange the filled cannelloni tubes in a single layer on top.
Pour the remaining passata over the tubes and scatter with the grated cheese.
Cover with foil and bake at 190°C for 25 minutes, then uncover and bake a further 10-15 minutes until the cheese is golden and the tubes are tender.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

Vegetable Pad See Ew.title: Vegetable Pad See Ew
method_steps: Cut the broccoli into small florets and thinly slice the carrot.
Gently separate the fresh rice noodles by hand if they are clumped together.
Heat a wok over high heat until just smoking, then add a little oil.
Add the garlic, broccoli and carrot and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until fragrant and tender-crisp.
Push the vegetables to one side, crack in the eggs and scramble until just set, about 1 minute.
Add the noodles, soy sauce, oyster sauce and sugar, tossing everything together over high heat and letting the noodles char slightly in places, about 3-4 minutes.
Toss once more to combine everything evenly.
Serve immediately while hot.

Sweet Potato & Black Bean Tacos.title: Sweet Potato & Black Bean Tacos
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray.
Peel and dice the sweet potato into 2cm cubes, then toss with the cumin, paprika, chilli powder, garlic powder, oregano and a little oil.
Spread the sweet potato on the tray and roast for 20-25 minutes, turning once, until tender and caramelised at the edges.
Meanwhile, drain and rinse the black beans and warm them in a small saucepan over medium heat for about 5 minutes.
Slice or roughly mash the avocado and squeeze over a little lime juice.
Warm the tortillas according to packet directions.
Fill the warm tortillas with the roasted sweet potato and black beans.
Top with avocado, extra lime juice and coriander, then serve immediately.

Vegetable Korma.title: Vegetable Korma
method_steps: Cook the rice according to packet directions and keep warm.
Dice the onion.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat and cook the onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the korma paste and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the frozen mixed vegetables and stir to coat well in the paste.
Pour in the cream and ground almonds and bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Season with salt, scatter with coriander and serve with the rice.

Butter Bean & Tomato Stew.title: Butter Bean & Tomato Stew
method_steps: Dice the onion and red capsicum, and mince the garlic.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion and capsicum until softened, about 6 minutes, then add the garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the smoked paprika and cook for 30 seconds to release its flavour.
Drain and rinse the butter beans, then add them to the pan with the tinned tomatoes.
Bring to a simmer, cover partially, and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened and the beans are tender.
Season with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls and serve hot with crusty bread.

Pumpkin Soup with Bread.title: Pumpkin Soup with Bread
method_steps: Peel and chop the pumpkin and potato into chunks, dice the onion, and mince the garlic.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the pumpkin, potato and vegetable stock, and bring to the boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes until the vegetables are very tender.
Remove from the heat and blend with a stick blender (or in batches in an upright blender) until smooth.
Return to low heat and stir through the cream and nutmeg, warming gently without letting it boil.
Season with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls and serve with crusty bread.

Caprese Pasta.title: Caprese Pasta
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the penne until al dente, about 11 minutes.
Meanwhile, halve the cherry tomatoes, tear the mozzarella into pieces, and tear the basil leaves.
Reserve a cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the pasta.
Return the hot pasta to the pot and add the cherry tomatoes, mozzarella and basil.
Warm the olive oil with the crushed garlic for 1 minute, then drizzle over the pasta and toss gently, letting the residual heat soften the mozzarella slightly.
Loosen with a splash of the reserved pasta water if the pasta looks dry.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve immediately while warm.

Roasted Vegetable Pasta.title: Roasted Vegetable Pasta
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Chop the capsicum, zucchini, eggplant and onion into 2-3cm chunks.
Toss the vegetables with oil, the garlic, dried oregano, salt and pepper, then spread on a baking tray.
Roast for 25-30 minutes, turning once, until tender and caramelised at the edges.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the penne until al dente, about 11 minutes.
Warm the tomato passata in a large pan over medium heat while the pasta finishes cooking.
Drain the pasta and add it to the passata along with the roasted vegetables, tossing to combine.
Tear over the basil leaves, scatter with parmesan if using, season with salt and pepper, and serve.

Tofu Katsu Curry.title: Tofu Katsu Curry
method_steps: Cook the rice according to packet directions and keep warm.
Peel and finely dice the onion and carrot for the curry sauce.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, garlic and ginger until softened, about 6-8 minutes.
Stir in the curry powder and cook for 1 minute until fragrant, then add the stock, soy sauce and honey and simmer for 10 minutes until the vegetables are soft and the sauce has thickened.
Meanwhile, press the tofu between paper towel to remove excess moisture, then cut into thick slabs.
Dust the tofu in the plain flour, dip in the beaten egg, then coat well in the panko breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat and fry the crumbed tofu for 2-3 minutes each side until golden and crisp.
Slice the tofu and serve over the rice, topped with the curry sauce.

Zucchini & Cheese Slice.title: Zucchini & Cheese Slice
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C and grease and line a slice tin.
Grate the zucchini, then squeeze out as much liquid as possible using a clean tea towel or paper towel.
Finely dice or grate the onion.
Whisk the eggs in a large bowl.
Add the zucchini, onion, self-raising flour and grated cheese to the eggs and mix until well combined.
Season with salt and pepper.
Pour the mixture into the prepared tin and spread evenly.
Bake at 180°C for 35 minutes until golden, set in the centre and a skewer comes out clean.
Rest for 5 minutes, then slice and serve with the salad leaves.

Spanish Omelette.title: Spanish Omelette
method_steps: Peel and thinly slice the potatoes, and thinly slice the onion.
Heat the olive oil in a medium non-stick frying pan over low-medium heat.
Add the potato and onion, cooking slowly and stirring occasionally, until very soft but not browned, about 15-18 minutes.
Meanwhile, whisk the eggs in a large bowl and season with salt and pepper.
Drain any excess oil from the potato mixture, then stir the potato and onion through the beaten eggs.
Return the mixture to the pan over medium heat and cook until the edges are set and the underside is golden, about 5-6 minutes.
Place a plate over the pan, carefully invert the omelette onto it, then slide it back into the pan to cook the other side, about 4-5 minutes, until just set in the centre.
Slide onto a plate, rest for a few minutes, then slice into wedges and serve with the salad leaves.

Baked Gnocchi with Vegetables.title: Baked Gnocchi with Vegetables
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced).
Heat the olive oil in a large ovenproof frying pan over medium heat and cook the garlic, zucchini and capsicum for 4–5 minutes, until just softened.
Pour in the tomato passata and dried oregano, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the gnocchi to the sauce and toss gently to coat.
Transfer to a baking dish if needed.
Scatter the grated mozzarella and parmesan evenly over the top.
Bake for 20 minutes, until the cheese is melted and golden and the sauce is bubbling at the edges.
Rest for a couple of minutes, then serve.

Dal Makhani.title: Dal Makhani
method_steps: Finely chop the onion, and grate or finely chop the ginger and garlic.
Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet instructions, then keep covered.
Melt half the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion for 4–5 minutes, until softened.
Add the ginger and garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the garam masala and chilli powder and cook for 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
Drain the lentils and kidney beans, stir them in, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft and the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt and pepper, and crumble in the kasuri methi if using.
Stir in the remaining butter and the thickened cream, and simmer for a further 2–3 minutes until glossy.
Serve the dal spooned over the rice, or with naan.

Margherita Pizza.title: Margherita Pizza
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced), placing a baking tray inside to heat up.
Spread the tomato passata evenly over both pizza bases, leaving a small border around the edge.
Drizzle a little of the olive oil over the passata.
Tear the fresh mozzarella into pieces and scatter over the bases.
Carefully transfer the bases onto the hot tray.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, until the crust is golden and crisp and the cheese is melted and bubbling.
Remove from the oven and scatter with fresh basil leaves and parmesan.
Slice and serve immediately.

BBQ Chicken Pizza.title: BBQ Chicken Pizza
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced), placing a baking tray inside to heat up.
Season the chicken breast and cook in a little olive oil in a hot pan or grill for 5–6 minutes each side, until cooked through and reaching 75°C in the centre.
Rest the chicken for a few minutes, then slice thinly.
Thinly slice the red onion.
Spread the barbecue sauce evenly over the pizza bases.
Top with the sliced chicken, red onion and grated mozzarella.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, until the base is golden and crisp and the cheese is melted.
Slice and serve hot.

Ham & Pineapple Pizza.title: Ham & Pineapple Pizza
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced).
Drain the tinned pineapple well and chop into small chunks.
Spread the tomato passata evenly over both pizza bases.
Tear or roughly chop the sliced ham and scatter over the bases along with the pineapple.
Top with the grated mozzarella.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, until the base is golden and crisp and the cheese is melted and bubbling.
Slice and serve immediately.

Vegetable Quesadillas.title: Vegetable Quesadillas
method_steps: Dice the red capsicum, slice the spring onion, and drain the tinned corn and black beans.
Heat a little oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the capsicum for 3–4 minutes, until softened.
Add the corn, black beans, ground cumin and smoked paprika, season with salt and pepper, and cook for 2–3 minutes, until heated through and fragrant.
Tip the filling into a bowl and wipe out the pan.
Lay half the tortillas on a board, top each with the vegetable mixture, spring onion and grated cheese, then place a second tortilla on top.
Toast the quesadillas in the pan over medium heat for 2–3 minutes each side, until golden and the cheese has melted.
Cut into wedges and serve warm, with sour cream if using.

Loaded Vegetable Nachos.title: Loaded Vegetable Nachos
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced).
Drain and rinse the kidney beans.

Heat a little oil in a saucepan over medium heat, add the beans, ground cumin, chilli powder, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and dried oregano, and cook for 3–4 minutes, stirring, until warmed through and fragrant.
Spread the corn chips over a large baking tray.
Spoon the spiced beans over the chips and scatter with the grated cheese.
Bake for 8 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbling.
Meanwhile, dice the tomato and avocado, and roughly chop the coriander.
Remove the nachos from the oven and top with the diced tomato, avocado, coriander and sour cream.
Serve immediately while hot.

Sausage Rolls with Salad.title: Sausage Rolls with Salad
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Finely dice the onion and crush the garlic.
In a bowl, combine the sausage mince, onion, garlic, breadcrumbs, egg and Worcestershire sauce, and season well with salt and pepper.
Lay out the puff pastry sheets and cut each in half.
Shape the mince mixture into logs along one edge of each pastry half, then roll up to enclose, sealing the edge with a little water.
Cut each roll into 3–4 pieces and place seam-side down on the tray.
Bake for 25 minutes, until the pastry is puffed and golden and the sausage mince is cooked through.
Rest for a few minutes.
Serve with a garden salad and tomato sauce on the side.

Fried Egg Rice Bowl.title: Fried Egg Rice Bowl
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions, or use leftover cooked rice.
Thinly slice the spring onion and crush the garlic.
Heat the sesame oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat, add the rice, garlic and soy sauce, and stir-fry for 2–3 minutes until heated through.
Divide the rice between 4 bowls.
Wipe out the pan, add a little oil, and fry the eggs over medium heat for 2–3 minutes, until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft.
Top each bowl with a fried egg.
Scatter with the sliced spring onion and drizzle with chilli sauce.
Serve immediately.

Minute Steak with Salad.title: Minute Steak with Salad
method_steps: Slice the tomato and cucumber, and toss with the mixed salad leaves in a large bowl.
Season the minute steaks well with salt and pepper.
Heat a little olive oil in a large frying pan over high heat until very hot.
Sear the steaks for 1–2 minutes each side, until browned and cooked to your liking.
Rest the steaks on a plate for 2–3 minutes.
Whisk the dijon mustard with a little olive oil to make a quick dressing, and toss through the salad.
Serve the steaks with the salad and mustard dressing alongside.

Chicken Caesar Wraps.title: Chicken Caesar Wraps
method_steps: Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat a grill pan or frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 6–7 minutes each side, until golden and cooked through, reaching 75°C in the centre.
Rest the chicken for a few minutes, then slice thinly.
Meanwhile, shred the cos lettuce and halve the cherry tomatoes.
Lay the tortillas on a board and divide the shredded cos lettuce among them.
Top with the sliced chicken and cherry tomatoes, drizzle with caesar dressing, and scatter with grated parmesan.
Roll up the wraps tightly, tucking in the sides as you go.
Slice in half and serve.

Beef Stir Fry with Black Bean Sauce.title: Beef Stir Fry with Black Bean Sauce
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Slice the capsicum and onion, and crush the garlic and grate the ginger.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until just smoking.
Stir-fry the beef strips in batches for 1–2 minutes, until browned, then remove and set aside.
Add a little more oil and stir-fry the capsicum, onion, garlic and ginger for 3–4 minutes, until softened and fragrant.
Return the beef to the wok, add the black bean sauce, and toss to combine.
Cook for a further 1–2 minutes, until the beef is heated through and coated in the sauce.
Serve immediately over the rice.

Chicken Cordon Bleu.title: Chicken Cordon Bleu
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Peel and chop the potatoes, then boil in salted water for 15–20 minutes, until tender, ready for mashing.
Meanwhile, slice a pocket into each chicken breast.
Fill each pocket with sliced ham and grated cheese, securing with a toothpick if needed.
Set up three shallow bowls: plain flour in one, whisked eggs in another, and breadcrumbs in the third, then coat each stuffed chicken breast in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs.
Place on the tray and bake for 25 minutes, until golden and cooked through, reaching 75°C in the centre.
Meanwhile, steam or boil the green beans for 3–4 minutes, until just tender.
Drain the potatoes and mash with the butter and milk until smooth, seasoning with salt and pepper.
Rest the chicken for a few minutes, then serve with the mash and green beans.

Chicken Enchiladas.title: Chicken Enchiladas
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced).
Finely dice the onion and crush the garlic.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the onion and garlic for 3–4 minutes, until softened.
Add the chicken thigh fillets and cook for 6–7 minutes each side, until cooked through.
Remove the chicken, shred with two forks, and toss with the ground cumin, chilli powder, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and dried oregano.
Return the shredded chicken to the pan with a splash of the diced tomatoes and stir to combine, cooking for 2 minutes.
Divide the chicken mixture among the tortillas, roll up, and place seam-side down in a baking dish.
Pour the remaining diced tomatoes over the top and scatter with the grated cheese.
Bake for 20 minutes, until the cheese is melted and golden and the enchiladas are heated through.
Rest for a few minutes, then serve topped with sour cream and coriander.

Butter Chicken Meatballs.title: Butter Chicken Meatballs
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
In a bowl, combine the chicken mince, breadcrumbs and egg, season with salt and pepper, and mix well.
Roll the mixture into meatballs, about the size of a golf ball, and place on the tray.
Bake for 15–18 minutes, until browned and cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Heat the butter chicken paste in a large saucepan over medium heat for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the tomato passata and coconut milk, and simmer for 8–10 minutes, until slightly thickened.
Add the baked meatballs to the sauce and simmer for a further 3–4 minutes, until coated and heated through.
Serve the meatballs and sauce over the rice, topped with fresh coriander.

Pork Belly with Greens.title: Pork Belly with Greens
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced).
Pat the pork belly dry, score the skin in a crosshatch pattern, and rub well with salt and the Chinese five-spice.
Place the pork on a rack in a roasting tray, skin-side up.
Roast for 30 minutes at 220°C, then reduce the heat to 160°C and continue roasting for 1.5 hours, until tender.
Increase the heat back to 220°C for a final 15–20 minutes, until the skin is crisp and crackled.
Rest the pork for 10 minutes before slicing.
Meanwhile, crush the garlic and halve the bok choy.
Heat the sesame oil in a hot pan and stir-fry the bok choy and garlic with the soy sauce for 2–3 minutes, until just wilted.
Slice the pork belly and serve with the stir-fried Asian greens.

Honey Mustard Pork Steaks.title: Honey Mustard Pork Steaks
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan-forced).
Peel and chop the potatoes and carrots into even chunks, toss with oil and seasoning, and roast for 20–25 minutes, until golden and tender.
Meanwhile, season the pork loin steaks with salt and pepper and crush the garlic.
