Heat a little oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the pork for 3–4 minutes each side, until golden and just cooked through.
Remove the pork and rest on a plate, loosely covered with foil.
Add the garlic, honey and wholegrain mustard to the same pan with a splash of water, and stir over low heat for 1–2 minutes, until glossy and coating the back of a spoon.
Return the pork to the pan and turn to coat in the sauce.
Serve the pork steaks with the pan sauce spooned over, alongside the roast vegetables.

Salmon Poke Bowl.title: Salmon Poke Bowl
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions, then spread on a tray to cool.
Meanwhile, cook the edamame in boiling water for 3–4 minutes, then drain and cool.
Cut the salmon fillets into small, even cubes.
Toss the salmon cubes with the soy sauce, sesame oil and rice vinegar, and set aside to marinate briefly.
Slice the cucumber and avocado, and thinly slice the spring onion.
Divide the cooled rice among 4 bowls.
Top each bowl with the marinated salmon, avocado, cucumber, edamame and spring onion.
Scatter with sesame seeds and serve immediately, drizzled with any remaining marinade.

Fish Burritos.title: Fish Burritos
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Drain and rinse the black beans, then warm through in a small saucepan over low heat.
Pat the fish fillets dry and coat evenly with the ground cumin, chilli powder, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and dried oregano.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the fish for 3–4 minutes each side, until it flakes easily with a fork.
Remove from the pan and flake into large chunks, then squeeze over the lime juice.
Shred the red cabbage and roughly chop the coriander.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or the microwave.
Fill each tortilla with rice, black beans, flaked fish, red cabbage, salsa, sour cream and coriander.
Roll up tightly and toast in a dry pan for 1–2 minutes each side, until golden and crisp.
Serve immediately.

Mushroom Stroganoff.title: Mushroom Stroganoff
method_steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the fettuccine according to packet instructions until al dente.
Meanwhile, finely slice the onion and mushrooms, and crush the garlic.
Melt the butter in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the onion for 4–5 minutes, until softened.
Add the mushrooms, garlic and smoked paprika, and cook for 5–6 minutes, until golden and any liquid has evaporated.
Pour in the vegetable stock and dijon mustard, and simmer for 5 minutes, until slightly reduced.
Reduce the heat to low, stir in the sour cream, and cook for 2 minutes, until smooth and coating the back of a spoon (do not boil).
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Drain the pasta and toss through the stroganoff sauce.
Serve immediately, scattered with extra paprika if desired.

Vegetable Jalfrezi.title: Vegetable Jalfrezi
method_steps: Chop the onion and capsicum into similar-sized pieces, then mince the garlic, ginger and chilli (if using).
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium heat and cook the onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the capsicum, garlic, ginger and chilli and cook for 2 minutes, stirring, until fragrant.
Stir in the curry powder and turmeric and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste, tinned tomatoes and frozen mixed vegetables and stir to combine.
Simmer uncovered for 15-18 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions and keep warm.
Taste the curry and season with salt and pepper as needed.
Spoon the jalfrezi over the rice and scatter with coriander leaves to serve.

Halloumi & Salad Wraps.title: Halloumi & Salad Wraps
method_steps: Slice the halloumi into 1 cm thick pieces.
Heat a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat (no oil needed, as halloumi releases enough moisture).
Pan-fry the halloumi for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and crisp.
Remove the halloumi from the pan and set aside.
Warm the tortillas briefly in the same pan or in the microwave for 20 seconds until pliable.
Spread each tortilla with a portion of the hummus.
Top with the mixed salad leaves, sliced cucumber and halved cherry tomatoes.
Add the warm halloumi slices on top, then fold or roll the wraps tightly and serve immediately.

Pea & Ham Soup.title: Pea & Ham Soup
method_steps: Roughly chop the onion, carrot and celery, and mince the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, celery and garlic for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the split peas, ham hock and bay leaves, then pour in enough water to cover everything (about 1.5 litres).
Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat and simmer uncovered for 50 minutes, skimming any froth, until the peas have broken down and the ham is falling off the bone.
Remove the ham hock from the pot and set aside to cool slightly.
Shred the ham meat from the bone, discarding the skin, fat and bone, and remove the bay leaves.
Stir the shredded ham back into the soup and simmer for a further 5 minutes to heat through.
Season with black pepper to taste, keeping in mind the ham hock adds saltiness, then ladle into bowls and serve with crusty bread.

Beef Kofta Curry.title: Beef Kofta Curry
method_steps: Finely dice one onion and mince the garlic and ginger, dividing the garlic and ginger between the koftas and the sauce.
In a bowl, combine the beef mince with half the diced onion, half the garlic and ginger, 1 tablespoon of the garam masala, the ground cumin, a little chopped coriander and a pinch of salt.
Roll the mixture into small kofta shapes, about the size of a golf ball.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat and brown the koftas for 4-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until coloured on all sides (they don't need to be cooked through yet).
Remove the koftas and set aside, then thinly slice the remaining onion and cook in the same pan for 3-4 minutes with the remaining garlic and ginger until softened.
Stir in the remaining garam masala and the tinned diced tomatoes and bring to a simmer.
Remove the pan from the heat briefly and stir through the natural yoghurt to prevent it splitting, then return to a gentle simmer.
Return the koftas to the sauce and simmer gently for 20 minutes until the beef reaches at least 71°C in the centre and the sauce has thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Serve the koftas and sauce spooned over the rice, scattered with the remaining coriander.

Teriyaki Tofu Bowl.title: Teriyaki Tofu Bowl
method_steps: Pat the firm tofu dry with paper towel and cut into 2 cm cubes.
Cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions and keep warm.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat and add the garlic and ginger.
Add the tofu and fry for 6-8 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and crisp on most sides.
Meanwhile, steam or microwave the broccoli florets for 3-4 minutes until just tender.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the tofu and toss gently to coat, letting it bubble for 1-2 minutes until glossy and sticky.
Divide the rice between bowls and top with the glazed tofu and steamed broccoli, then scatter with sesame seeds and sliced spring onion to serve.

Grilled Chicken & Garden Salad.title: Grilled Chicken & Garden Salad
method_steps: Pat the chicken breast dry, rub with a little of the olive oil and season both sides with salt and pepper.
Preheat a grill pan or barbecue to medium-high heat.
Grill the chicken for 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 75°C.
Rest the chicken on a plate for 5 minutes, then slice thickly on an angle.
Meanwhile, slice the cucumber and tomato and thinly slice the red onion.
In a large bowl, toss the mixed salad leaves, cucumber, tomato and red onion together.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the salad and drizzle with the remaining olive oil, then toss to coat.
Top the salad with the sliced chicken and serve straight away.

Lemon Garlic Prawn Salad.title: Lemon Garlic Prawn Salad
method_steps: Peel and devein the prawns, then finely mince the garlic.
Wash and dry the mixed salad leaves and halve the cherry tomatoes.
Slice the avocado just before serving to prevent browning.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant, then add the prawns in a single layer.
Pan-fry the prawns for 2-3 minutes per side until pink, opaque and just cooked through.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the prawns in the last minute of cooking and toss to combine.
Arrange the salad leaves, avocado and cherry tomatoes on plates and top with the warm garlic prawns to serve.

Zucchini Noodle Chicken Stir Fry.title: Zucchini Noodle Chicken Stir Fry
method_steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and mince the garlic and ginger.
Spiralise or slice the zucchini into noodle-like strips and cut the capsicum into thin strips.
Heat the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat.
Stir-fry the chicken strips for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through, then remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the zucchini noodles and capsicum to the pan and stir-fry for 3 minutes until just tender but still with some bite.
Return the chicken to the pan along with the garlic, ginger and soy sauce.
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes, then drizzle with the sesame oil.
Serve immediately while hot, so the zucchini noodles stay crisp.

Steamed Fish with Asian Greens.title: Steamed Fish with Asian Greens
method_steps: Grate the ginger, mince the garlic and pat the fish fillets dry.
Place the fish fillets in a heatproof dish, scatter with the ginger and garlic, and drizzle with half the soy sauce.
Bring a wok or large pot of water to a simmer and set a steamer over the top, ensuring the water doesn't touch the fish.
Steam the fish, covered, for 8-10 minutes until it flakes easily with a fork and reaches 63°C in the centre.
Meanwhile, trim the bok choy and separate the leaves, then steam alongside the fish for the final 3-4 minutes until just wilted.
Remove the fish and bok choy from the steamer and scatter with the sliced spring onion.
Heat the vegetable oil and sesame oil in a small pan until just smoking, then carefully pour over the fish and spring onion.
Drizzle with the remaining soy sauce and serve immediately.

Chicken & Cabbage Stir Fry.title: Chicken & Cabbage Stir Fry
method_steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and mince the garlic and ginger.
Shred the cabbage finely and grate or julienne the carrot.
Heat the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until shimmering.
Stir-fry the chicken strips for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through, then remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the shredded cabbage and carrot to the hot pan and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until just softened but still crunchy.
Return the chicken to the pan along with the garlic, ginger and soy sauce.
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve immediately while hot.

Vegetable & White Bean Soup.title: Vegetable & White Bean Soup
method_steps: Finely dice the onion, carrot and celery, and mince the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, celery, garlic and dried oregano for 5-6 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Drain and rinse the tinned cannellini beans.
Pour in the vegetable stock and tinned diced tomatoes and stir to combine.
Add the cannellini beans and bring the soup to a simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes until the vegetables are tender and the flavours have developed.
Stir through the baby spinach in the last 2 minutes of cooking until just wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste and ladle into bowls to serve.

Tuna Salad Lettuce Cups.title: Tuna Salad Lettuce Cups
method_steps: Drain the tinned tuna and tinned corn well.
Finely dice the red onion.
In a mixing bowl, combine the tuna, corn and red onion.
Drizzle with the olive oil and squeeze in the lemon juice.
Season with salt and pepper and mix gently, being careful not to break up the tuna too much.
Separate the baby cos lettuce into individual leaves, washing and patting them dry, to form cups.
Spoon the tuna mixture into the lettuce cups.
Serve immediately, with extra lemon wedges on the side if desired.

Cauliflower Rice Chicken Bowl.title: Cauliflower Rice Chicken Bowl
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh into bite-sized pieces and toss with the cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder and chilli powder.
Roughly chop the cauliflower into florets, then pulse in a food processor until it resembles rice grains (or use a box grater).

Heat half the vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the spiced chicken for 6-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and cooked through, reaching 75°C in the centre.
Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside, keeping it warm.
Add the remaining oil to the pan and stir-fry the cauliflower rice for 4-5 minutes until tender and lightly golden.
Season the cauliflower rice with salt and pepper to taste.
Slice the avocado and dice the tomato.
Divide the cauliflower rice between bowls and top with the chicken, avocado and tomato, then squeeze over the lime juice to serve.

Miso Grilled Salmon with Greens.title: Miso Grilled Salmon with Greens
method_steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Whisk together the miso paste, mirin, soy sauce and brown sugar, then pat the salmon dry and brush both sides generously with the miso glaze.
Place the salmon on a lined tray, skin-side down, and grill for 8-10 minutes until the top is caramelised and the fish flakes easily, reaching about 63°C in the centre for a just-cooked result.
Meanwhile, trim the broccolini.
Steam the broccolini and edamame together for 4-5 minutes until the broccolini is bright green and tender-crisp.
Rest the salmon for 1-2 minutes before serving.
Divide the broccolini and edamame between plates alongside the miso salmon to serve.

Poached Chicken & Slaw.title: Poached Chicken & Slaw
method_steps: Place the chicken breast in a saucepan and cover with water.
Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat and poach for 15-18 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and reaches 75°C in the centre.
Remove the chicken from the poaching liquid, rest for 5 minutes, then shred using two forks.
Meanwhile, finely shred the cabbage and grate the carrot.
Pick the mint and coriander leaves and roughly chop, and finely slice the chilli (if using).
In a large bowl, toss the cabbage, carrot, mint and coriander together.
Whisk together the lime juice, fish sauce, brown sugar, minced garlic and chilli to make a dressing, then pour over the slaw and toss to combine.
Add the shredded chicken to the slaw and toss gently to combine.
Scatter with the crushed roasted peanuts to serve.

Prawn & Vegetable Skewers.title: Prawn & Vegetable Skewers
method_steps: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 20 minutes to prevent burning.
Peel and devein the prawns, and cut the capsicum and zucchini into chunks similar in size to the prawns.
Mince the garlic and combine with the olive oil to make a basting mixture.
Thread the prawns, capsicum and zucchini alternately onto the skewers.
Preheat a barbecue or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill the skewers for 8 minutes, turning occasionally and brushing with the garlic oil, until the prawns are pink and opaque and the vegetables are lightly charred.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the skewers as soon as they come off the heat.
Serve the skewers alongside the mixed salad leaves.

Spiced Chickpea & Spinach Stew.title: Spiced Chickpea & Spinach Stew
method_steps: Finely dice the onion and mince the garlic.
Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, ground cumin, smoked paprika and ground coriander and cook for 1 minute, stirring, until fragrant.
Add the chickpeas and tinned diced tomatoes and stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and coats the back of a spoon.
Stir through the baby spinach in the last 2 minutes of cooking until just wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste and serve hot.

Grilled Fish Lettuce Tacos.title: Grilled Fish Lettuce Tacos
method_steps: Pat the white fish fillets dry and coat evenly with the cumin, paprika, garlic powder, dried oregano and chilli powder (if using).
Finely shred the cabbage and dice the tomato and red onion for a quick slaw and salsa.
Heat the olive oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
Pan-fry the fish for 3-4 minutes per side until it flakes easily with a fork and reaches 63°C in the centre.
Remove the fish from the pan and flake into large chunks using two forks.
Separate the iceberg lettuce into individual leaves to use as taco cups.
Slice the avocado.
Fill the lettuce cups with the flaked fish, cabbage slaw, diced tomato and red onion, and avocado, then squeeze over the lime juice and scatter with coriander to serve.

Chicken Zoodle Soup.title: Chicken Zoodle Soup
method_steps: Dice the chicken breast, slice the carrot and celery, and spiralise or slice the zucchini into noodles.
Pour the chicken stock into a large pot and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast, carrot, celery, garlic and bay leaf to the simmering stock.
Cook for 20-25 minutes until the chicken is cooked through, reaching 75°C, and the vegetables are tender.
Skim any froth from the surface as needed for a clearer soup.
Add the zucchini noodles in the final 2-3 minutes of cooking, stirring gently, so they stay slightly firm.
Remove the bay leaf and season the soup with salt and pepper to taste.
Ladle into bowls, scatter with chopped parsley and serve hot.

Roasted Vegetable & Lentil Salad.title: Roasted Vegetable & Lentil Salad
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Cut the pumpkin into 2 cm cubes, slice the red capsicum, and cut the red onion into wedges.
Spread the vegetables on a lined baking tray, drizzle with 2 tablespoons of the olive oil and sprinkle with the ground cumin.
Roast for 25-30 minutes, turning halfway, until the vegetables are golden and tender.
Meanwhile, drain and rinse the tinned brown lentils.
Whisk together the lemon juice with the remaining tablespoon of olive oil, salt and pepper to make a dressing.
In a large bowl, combine the roasted vegetables, lentils and rocket.
Drizzle with the lemon dressing and toss gently to combine before serving.

Baked Cod with Ratatouille.title: Baked Cod with Ratatouille
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Dice the eggplant, zucchini, red capsicum and onion into even 2cm pieces, and finely chop the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large ovenproof frying pan over medium heat and cook the onion for 3 minutes until softened.
Add the eggplant, zucchini, capsicum and garlic, and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly golden, about 8 minutes.
Add the tinned tomatoes and dried oregano, season with salt and pepper, and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes until the ratatouille has thickened slightly.
Season the cod fillets, then nestle them on top of the ratatouille.
Transfer the pan to the oven and bake for 15 minutes, until the cod is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Rest for 2 minutes, then scatter with torn basil leaves and spoon the ratatouille and pan juices over each piece of cod to serve.

Vietnamese Chicken Salad.title: Vietnamese Chicken Salad
method_steps: Place the chicken breast in a saucepan, cover with water and bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat; poach for 12-15 minutes until cooked through and reaching 75°C.
Remove the chicken and rest for 5 minutes, then shred into bite-sized pieces using two forks.
While the chicken poaches, finely shred the cabbage, grate the carrot, and pick and roughly chop the mint and coriander leaves.
Finely chop the garlic, then whisk together the fish sauce, lime juice, sugar and garlic in a small bowl to make the dressing.
In a large bowl, toss the cabbage, carrot, mint, coriander and shredded chicken with the dressing until well combined.
Roughly chop the peanuts.
Divide the salad among bowls, scatter with peanuts and fried shallots, if using, to serve.

Mushroom & Spinach Omelette.title: Mushroom & Spinach Omelette
method_steps: Slice the button mushrooms.
Crack the eggs into a bowl, season with salt and pepper, and whisk until combined.
Heat the olive oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium heat and cook the mushrooms, stirring, until golden and softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the baby spinach and cook, stirring, until just wilted, about 1 minute.
Pour the beaten eggs evenly over the mushrooms and spinach, tilting the pan to coat the base.
Cook for 2-3 minutes until the edges are set, then use a spatula to fold the omelette in half.
Cook for a further 1-2 minutes until just set in the centre, then slide onto a plate.
Serve with the mixed salad leaves alongside.

Chicken San Choy Bau.title: Chicken San Choy Bau
method_steps: Separate the iceberg lettuce leaves into cups, wash and pat dry, then set aside.
Finely chop the garlic and ginger, slice the spring onion, and drain and chop the tinned water chestnuts.
Heat the sesame oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat and add the chicken mince, breaking it up with a spoon.
Stir-fry the mince until browned and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger and water chestnuts and stir-fry for a further 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the hoisin sauce and soy sauce and toss to coat everything evenly, then season to taste.
Remove from the heat, stir through the spring onion, and spoon the mixture into the lettuce cups.
Serve immediately while warm.

Cabbage & Chicken Rice-Paper Rolls.title: Cabbage & Chicken Rice-Paper Rolls
method_steps: Place the chicken breast in a saucepan, cover with water and bring to a gentle simmer; poach for 12-15 minutes until cooked through and reaching 75°C.
Remove the chicken, rest for 5 minutes, then slice thinly.
Meanwhile, cook the rice vermicelli noodles according to the packet instructions, then drain and rinse under cold water.
Finely shred the cabbage, and julienne the carrot and cucumber into thin matchsticks; pick the mint and coriander leaves.
Fill a wide shallow bowl with warm water.
Dip one sheet of rice paper into the water for about 10 seconds until just pliable, then lay it on a clean damp tea towel.
Arrange a little chicken, noodles, cabbage, carrot, cucumber and herbs across the lower third of the sheet.
Fold in the sides and roll up tightly to enclose the filling, repeating with the remaining rice paper and filling.
Serve the rolls with the hoisin sauce and chopped peanuts for dipping.

Grilled Chicken with Cauliflower Mash.title: Grilled Chicken with Cauliflower Mash
method_steps: Cut the cauliflower into florets and finely chop the garlic.
Steam the cauliflower over a pot of simmering water until very tender, about 10-12 minutes.
Trim the green beans.
Transfer the steamed cauliflower to a blender or food processor with the garlic, vegetable stock and olive oil, and blend until smooth, seasoning with salt and pepper.
Season the chicken breast and heat a grill pan or barbecue over medium-high heat.
Grill the chicken for 6-7 minutes each side, until cooked through and reaching 75°C.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes before slicing.
Steam or blanch the green beans until bright green and tender-crisp, about 3-4 minutes.
Serve the sliced chicken over the cauliflower mash with the green beans alongside.

Prawn & Zucchini Stir Fry.title: Prawn & Zucchini Stir Fry
method_steps: Slice the zucchini and red capsicum, and finely chop the garlic and ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat.
Add the prawns and stir-fry until just pink and curled, about 2-3 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Add the zucchini and capsicum to the wok and stir-fry until tender-crisp, about 4 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and stir-fry for a further 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the prawns to the wok along with the soy sauce and sesame oil and toss to combine and heat through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Chicken & Broccoli Soup.title: Chicken & Broccoli Soup
method_steps: Dice the onion and carrot, and finely chop the garlic.
Heat a little oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken stock and chicken breast, bring to a simmer, and cook for 12-15 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and reaches 75°C.
Remove the chicken, rest briefly, then shred with two forks.
Chop the broccoli into small florets and add to the pot, simmering until tender, about 8 minutes.
Ladle half the soup into a blender (or use a stick blender) and blend until smooth for added body.
Return the blended soup and shredded chicken to the pot and stir to combine.
Season to taste and simmer for a further 2 minutes before serving hot.

Baked Salmon with Asparagus.title: Baked Salmon with Asparagus
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray.
Trim the woody ends from the asparagus and finely chop the garlic.
Arrange the salmon fillets and asparagus on the tray, scatter with garlic, drizzle with the olive oil, and season with salt and pepper.
Slice the lemon and tuck a few slices among the salmon and asparagus.
Roast for 15 minutes, until the salmon flakes easily and the asparagus is tender.
Rest the salmon for 2 minutes, then scatter with chopped dill.
Serve with the mixed salad leaves and remaining lemon wedges.

Mixed Bean & Vegetable Chilli.title: Mixed Bean & Vegetable Chilli
method_steps: Dice the onion and red capsicum, and finely chop the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, ground cumin, smoked paprika, dried oregano, ground coriander and chilli powder, and cook, stirring, for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the capsicum and cook for 3-4 minutes until starting to soften.
Drain and rinse the tinned mixed beans, then add them to the pan with the diced tomatoes and tomato paste.
Bring to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the rice according to the packet instructions.
Season the chilli to taste with salt and pepper and serve over the rice.

Steak & Eggs.title: Steak & Eggs
method_steps: Remove the steaks from the fridge 10 minutes before cooking, pat dry, and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a heavy-based frying pan over high heat until very hot.
Sear the steaks for 2-3 minutes each side for medium-rare (internal temperature about 54°C), or longer to your liking.
Transfer the steaks to a plate, tent with foil, and rest for 5 minutes.
Halve the tomatoes and cook cut-side down in the same pan for 2-3 minutes until softened and lightly charred.
Add the baby spinach to the pan and cook briefly until just wilted.
Wipe out the pan if needed, then fry the eggs to your liking.
Slice the rested steaks and serve with the fried eggs, grilled tomato and spinach.

Chicken & Quinoa Power Bowl.title: Chicken & Quinoa Power Bowl
method_steps: Rinse the quinoa well under cold water.
Cook the quinoa according to the packet instructions until tender and fluffy, about 15 minutes, then set aside to cool slightly.
Season the chicken breast and heat a grill pan or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Grill the chicken for 6-7 minutes each side, until cooked through and reaching 75°C.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes, then slice.
Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas, halve the cherry tomatoes, and slice the avocado.
Whisk the lemon juice and olive oil together to make a dressing.
Divide the quinoa among bowls and top with the sliced chicken, chickpeas, tomatoes, avocado and baby spinach, then drizzle with the lemon dressing to serve.

Beef & Black Bean Bowl.title: Beef & Black Bean Bowl
method_steps: Cook the rice according to the packet instructions.
Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat and add the beef mince, breaking it up with a spoon.
Brown the mince, stirring occasionally, for about 6-8 minutes until cooked through.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and chilli powder, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant, adding a splash of water if the pan looks dry.
Drain and rinse the tinned black beans and warm through in a small saucepan.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the spiced mince, black beans and salsa.
Squeeze over the lime juice and scatter with coriander to serve.

Salmon with Lentils.title: Salmon with Lentils
method_steps: Dice the onion and finely chop the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Drain the tinned lentils and add to the pan with the dried thyme and a splash of water, simmering for 10 minutes and mashing lightly with a spoon for a thick, saucy texture.
Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Heat a little oil in a separate frying pan over medium-high heat and cook the salmon skin-side down for 4 minutes until crisp.
Flip and cook for a further 2-3 minutes until just cooked through.
Rest the salmon for 1-2 minutes.
Serve the salmon on top of the lentils with a squeeze of lemon.

Chicken Souvlaki Bowl.title: Chicken Souvlaki Bowl
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillet into 3cm cubes and place in a bowl with the lemon juice and zest, chopped garlic, dried oregano and olive oil; toss to coat and set aside to marinate for 10-15 minutes.
Cook the rice according to the packet instructions.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers if using.
Heat a grill pan or barbecue over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until charred and cooked through, reaching 75°C.
Rest the chicken for a few minutes.
Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas, dice the cucumber, halve the cherry tomatoes, and thinly slice the red onion.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the chicken, chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, red onion and olives.

Prawn & Egg Fried Rice.title: Prawn & Egg Fried Rice
method_steps: Cook the rice according to the packet instructions, then spread on a tray and cool completely (day-old rice works best).
Beat the eggs in a bowl.
Heat a little vegetable oil in a wok over high heat, pour in the eggs, and scramble until just set; remove and set aside.
Add a little more oil to the wok and stir-fry the prawns, garlic and half the spring onion until the prawns are pink and curled, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the frozen peas and cook for 1-2 minutes until heated through.
Add the cooked rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Add the soy sauce and sesame oil and toss to coat evenly.
Fold the scrambled egg back through the rice and scatter with the remaining spring onion to serve.

Chicken Chilli (High-Protein).title: Chicken Chilli (High-Protein)
method_steps: Dice the onion and finely chop the garlic.
Heat a little oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat and add the chicken mince, breaking it up with a spoon.
Brown the mince for 6-7 minutes, then add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, dried oregano and chilli powder and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the kidney beans and diced tomatoes, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for 25 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the rice according to the packet instructions.
Season the chilli to taste and serve over the rice.

Beef Rump with Greens & Beans.title: Beef Rump with Greens & Beans
method_steps: Remove the beef rump from the fridge 10 minutes before cooking, pat dry, and season well with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a heavy-based frying pan over high heat until very hot.
Sear the rump with the rosemary sprig for 4-5 minutes each side for medium-rare (internal temperature about 54-57°C), or longer to your liking.
Transfer to a plate, tent with foil, and rest for 8 minutes.
Finely chop the garlic and trim the broccolini.
Steam or blanch the broccolini until bright green and tender-crisp, about 3 minutes.
In the same pan, warm the cannellini beans with the garlic over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Slice the rested rump against the grain and serve with the warm beans and broccolini.

Lamb & Chickpea Bowl.title: Lamb & Chickpea Bowl
method_steps: Prepare the couscous according to the packet instructions and fluff with a fork.
Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat and add the lamb mince, breaking it up with a spoon.
Brown the mince for 6-7 minutes until cooked through.
Stir in the ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, ground cinnamon, ground turmeric and ground ginger, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas and add to the pan, warming through for 2-3 minutes.
Pick and roughly chop the mint leaves.
Whisk the lemon juice and olive oil together to make a dressing.
Divide the couscous among bowls, top with the spiced lamb and chickpeas, then drizzle with the lemon dressing and scatter with mint to serve.

Tofu & Edamame Stir Fry.title: Tofu & Edamame Stir Fry
method_steps: Pat the tofu dry with paper towel and cut into 2cm cubes.
Cook the rice according to the packet instructions.
Slice the red capsicum and finely chop the garlic and ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat and fry the tofu, turning occasionally, until golden and crisp on all sides, about 6-8 minutes; remove and set aside.
Add a little more oil to the wok and stir-fry the capsicum, garlic and ginger until tender-crisp, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the edamame and cook for 1-2 minutes until heated through.
Return the tofu to the wok, add the soy sauce and sesame oil, and toss to coat evenly.
Serve the stir-fry over the cooked rice.

Chicken & Bean Burrito Bowl.title: Chicken & Bean Burrito Bowl
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice, then cook in a saucepan with water according to packet directions until tender; rest covered.
Combine the ground cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano and chilli powder in a small bowl.
Pat the chicken breast dry, slice into even strips, and toss with the spice mix to coat.
Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat with a little oil and cook the chicken strips for 4-5 minutes each side, until golden and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C).
Rest the chicken for 3 minutes, then slice.
Warm the black beans and drained corn kernels together in a small saucepan over medium heat for 3-4 minutes.
Divide the rice between bowls and top with the beans, corn, sliced chicken and a spoonful of salsa.
Squeeze over the lime juice and scatter with fresh coriander.
Serve immediately while warm.

Salmon Poke Bowl (Protein).title: Salmon Poke Bowl (Protein)
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender; spread on a tray to cool slightly.
Pat the salmon fillets dry and cut into 2cm cubes.
Combine the soy sauce, sesame oil and rice vinegar in a bowl, add the salmon, and toss gently to coat.
Cover and marinate the salmon in the fridge for 10 minutes.
Cook the edamame in a small saucepan of boiling water for 2-3 minutes, until bright green and tender, then drain.
Halve the avocado, remove the stone, and slice the flesh; thinly slice the cucumber.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the marinated salmon, edamame, avocado and cucumber.
Drizzle over any remaining marinade and scatter with sesame seeds.
Serve immediately while the salmon is fresh and cold.

Chicken Shawarma Bowl.title: Chicken Shawarma Bowl
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Combine the ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, turmeric, garlic powder, cinnamon and cayenne pepper in a small bowl.
Pat the chicken thigh fillets dry, place in a bowl, and toss with the spice mix and a squeeze of lemon juice until well coated.
Arrange the chicken on the prepared tray and roast for 20-25 minutes, turning once, until golden and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C).
Meanwhile, rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas, then warm in a small saucepan over medium heat for 4-5 minutes.
Dice the tomatoes and cucumber.
Whisk the tahini with the remaining lemon juice and 2-3 tablespoons of water until smooth and pourable.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes, then slice; divide the rice among bowls and top with chicken, chickpeas, tomato and cucumber.
Drizzle with the tahini sauce and serve warm.

Baked Chicken Thighs & Lentils.title: Baked Chicken Thighs & Lentils
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C (fan-forced).
Pat the chicken thigh cutlets dry and season with salt and pepper.
Heat a large ovenproof frying pan or flameproof casserole dish over medium-high heat with a little oil and brown the chicken cutlets for 3-4 minutes each side, until golden, then remove from the pan.
Finely dice the onion and chop the garlic; add to the same pan and cook for 4-5 minutes, until softened.
Drain and rinse the brown lentils, then add to the pan along with the diced tomatoes, chicken stock and dried thyme, stirring to combine.
Nestle the browned chicken cutlets back into the lentil mixture, skin-side up.
Transfer to the oven and bake for 30 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C) and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Rest for a few minutes before serving, spooning the sauce over the chicken.

Steak Fajita Bowl.title: Steak Fajita Bowl
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender.
Thinly slice the red capsicum and onion.
Combine the cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, chilli powder and oregano in a small bowl, then toss the beef strips through the spice mix until evenly coated.
Heat a large frying pan or wok over high heat with a little oil until just smoking.
Sear the beef strips in batches for 1-2 minutes, until browned but still tender, then remove from the pan.
Add the capsicum and onion to the same pan and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes, until softened and lightly charred.
Return the beef to the pan and toss through for 1 minute to combine and heat through.
Warm the black beans in a small saucepan for 3-4 minutes.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the beef, vegetables and black beans.
Squeeze over the lime juice and scatter with fresh coriander to serve.

Prawn & Quinoa Salad.title: Prawn & Quinoa Salad
method_steps: Rinse the quinoa well, then cook in a saucepan with water according to packet directions for 12-15 minutes, until tender and the liquid is absorbed; set aside to cool.
Peel and devein the prawns.
Heat a frying pan over medium-high heat with a little oil and pan-fry the prawns for 1-2 minutes each side, until pink and just cooked through.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas, dice the tomatoes and cucumber, and roughly chop the parsley.
Combine the cooled quinoa, prawns, chickpeas, tomato, cucumber and parsley in a large bowl.
Whisk the olive oil with the juice of the lemon, then pour over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.

Chicken & Chickpea Curry.title: Chicken & Chickpea Curry
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillets into bite-sized pieces.
Finely dice the onion and chop the garlic.
Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender.
Heat a large saucepan over medium-high heat with a little oil and brown the chicken pieces for 4-5 minutes, until golden on all sides; remove and set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the same pan and cook for 3-4 minutes, until softened.
Add the curry paste and stir for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the pan, then drain and rinse the chickpeas and add with the diced tomatoes and coconut milk, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and cook uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Season to taste with salt and pepper and scatter with coriander leaves.
Serve the curry spooned over the rice.

Beef Kofta & Salad.title: Beef Kofta & Salad
method_steps: Preheat a grill or barbecue to medium-high heat.
Grate half the onion and finely chop the garlic and parsley.
Combine the beef mince, cumin, coriander, paprika, grated onion, garlic and parsley in a bowl, season with salt and pepper, and mix well.
Divide the mixture into 8 portions and shape each into an oval patty or mould onto a skewer to form koftas.
Grill the koftas for 3-4 minutes each side, turning occasionally, until browned and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 71°C).
Rest the koftas for a few minutes while you prepare the salad.
Dice the tomatoes and cucumber and thinly slice the remaining onion half; toss together in a bowl.
Divide the salad among plates and top with the koftas.
Serve with a generous spoonful of hummus on the side.

Grilled Fish & White Beans.title: Grilled Fish & White Beans
method_steps: Pat the fish fillets dry and season with salt and pepper.
Finely chop the garlic.
Drain and rinse the cannellini beans.
Heat 1 tablespoon of the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat and cook the garlic for 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Add the cannellini beans and cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
Stir through the baby spinach and cook for 1-2 minutes, until just wilted; season to taste.
Meanwhile, heat the remaining olive oil in a grill pan or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Grill the fish fillets for 3-4 minutes each side, until opaque and flaking easily with a fork.
Spoon the warm beans and spinach onto plates and top with the grilled fish.
Squeeze over the lemon juice, scatter with parsley, and serve immediately.

Chicken Meatballs & Quinoa.title: Chicken Meatballs & Quinoa
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (fan-forced) and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Finely chop the garlic.
Combine the chicken mince, breadcrumbs, egg, garlic, dried oregano and dried basil in a bowl, season with salt and pepper, and mix well.
Roll the mixture into 16-20 even meatballs.
Arrange the meatballs on the prepared tray and bake for 15 minutes, until lightly golden.
Meanwhile, rinse the quinoa and cook in a saucepan according to packet directions, about 12-15 minutes, until tender.
Heat the tomato passata in a large saucepan over medium heat until it just begins to simmer.
Add the baked meatballs to the passata and simmer gently for 10 minutes, until cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Fluff the cooked quinoa with a fork and divide among bowls.
Top with the meatballs and sauce and serve hot.

Turkey-Style Chicken Burgers (Protein).title: Turkey-Style Chicken Burgers (Protein)
method_steps: Season the chicken mince with salt and pepper in a bowl and mix well.
Divide the mixture into 4 portions and shape into patties about 1.5cm thick.
Heat a grill pan or frying pan over medium heat with a little oil.
Cook the patties for 5-6 minutes each side, until golden and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C).
Rest the patties for 2-3 minutes.
Meanwhile, slice the tomato and red onion, and split the wholemeal burger buns.
Lightly toast the cut sides of the buns in the same pan for 1 minute, until golden.
Spread the bun bases with mustard, then layer with salad leaves, a patty, tomato and red onion.
Top with the bun lids and serve immediately.

Egg & Bean Breakfast-for-Dinner.title: Egg & Bean Breakfast-for-Dinner
method_steps: Heat the baked beans in a saucepan over medium heat.
Halve the cherry tomatoes and stir through the beans along with the smoked paprika; simmer for 5-6 minutes, until warmed through and slightly thickened.
Meanwhile, heat a non-stick frying pan over medium heat with a little oil.
Crack the eggs into the pan and fry for 2-3 minutes, until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft (or poach in gently simmering water for 3 minutes).
While the eggs cook, toast the wholemeal bread slices until golden and thinly slice the spring onion.
Season the eggs with salt and pepper.
Divide the toast among plates and spoon over the smoky baked beans and tomatoes.
Top each with a fried or poached egg, scatter with spring onion, and serve immediately while hot.

Pork & Bean Stew (Protein).title: Pork & Bean Stew (Protein)
method_steps: Trim the pork loin and cut into 2-3cm cubes.
Finely dice the onion and carrot, and chop the garlic.
Heat a large saucepan or casserole dish over medium-high heat with a little oil.
Brown the pork in batches for 3-4 minutes, until golden on all sides, then remove from the pan.
Add the onion, carrot and garlic to the same pan and cook for 4-5 minutes, until softened.
Stir in the smoked paprika and cook for 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Return the pork to the pan, then add the drained cannellini beans, diced tomatoes and chicken stock, stirring to combine.
Bring to the boil, then reduce heat to low, cover and simmer for 35 minutes, until the pork is tender.
Season to taste with salt and pepper and serve hot, ladled into bowls.

Chicken & Lentil Soup (Protein).title: Chicken & Lentil Soup (Protein)
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillets into small pieces.
Finely dice the onion, carrot and celery.
Rinse the red lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
Heat a large saucepan or stockpot over medium-high heat with a little oil and brown the chicken for 3-4 minutes, until lightly golden.
Add the onion, carrot and celery and cook for 4-5 minutes, until softened.
Stir in the red lentils and pour in the chicken stock.
Bring to the boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 25 minutes, until the lentils are soft and the soup has thickened.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls, scatter with parsley, and serve hot.

Tuna & White Bean Salad.title: Tuna & White Bean Salad
method_steps: Drain the tinned tuna and cannellini beans well.
Thinly slice the red onion, dice the tomatoes, and roughly chop the parsley.
Flake the tuna into a large bowl using a fork.
Add the cannellini beans, red onion, tomato and parsley to the bowl.
Whisk the olive oil with the juice of the lemon.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine, being careful not to break up the tuna too much.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve immediately, or chill for 10 minutes for a cooler salad.

Chicken Teriyaki Donburi.title: Chicken Teriyaki Donburi
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender.
Pat the chicken thigh fillets dry and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat a large frying pan over medium-high heat with a little oil.
Pan-fry the chicken for 5-6 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and cooked through.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the chicken and simmer for 2-3 minutes, turning to coat, until it thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Thinly slice the spring onion.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the glazed chicken, spooning over any extra sauce from the pan.
Scatter over the sliced spring onion and sesame seeds and serve immediately.

Beef Bulgogi Bowl.title: Beef Bulgogi Bowl
method_steps: Finely chop or crush the garlic and grate the ginger.
Combine the beef strips, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, brown sugar and sesame oil in a bowl and toss to coat.
Cover and marinate in the fridge for at least 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender.
Slice the cucumber into thin batons and thinly slice the spring onion.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat until just smoking.
Sear the marinated beef in batches for 1-2 minutes, until browned but still tender, being careful not to overcrowd the pan.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the seared beef, cucumber and spring onion.
Scatter with sesame seeds and serve immediately while the beef is hot.

Pork Bibimbap.title: Pork Bibimbap
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender.
Julienne or grate the carrot into thin strips and chop the garlic.
Heat a little of the sesame oil in a frying pan over medium heat and stir-fry the carrot for 1-2 minutes, until just tender, then remove and set aside.
In the same pan, stir-fry the bean sprouts and baby spinach with a splash of soy sauce for 1-2 minutes, until just wilted, then remove and set aside.
Add the pork mince and garlic to the pan and cook over medium-high heat for 5-6 minutes, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
Stir the gochujang through the pork and cook for 1-2 minutes, until well coated and fragrant.
Wipe out the pan, then fry the eggs over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft.
Divide the rice among bowls and arrange the pork, carrot, spinach and bean sprouts in separate sections on top.
Top each bowl with a fried egg and serve immediately, stirring everything together before eating.

Singapore Noodles.title: Singapore Noodles
method_steps: Place the rice vermicelli in a large bowl, cover with boiling water and soak for 5-8 minutes, until softened, then drain.
Peel and devein the prawns, and slice the ham and capsicum into thin strips.
Whisk the eggs in a small bowl.
Heat a wok over high heat with a little oil until just smoking.
Pour in the egg and cook for 1 minute, until just set, then break it up and remove from the wok.
Add a little more oil to the wok, then stir-fry the prawns for 1-2 minutes, until pink and almost cooked through.
Add the ham and capsicum and stir-fry for 2 minutes, until the capsicum is just tender.
Sprinkle in the curry powder and stir-fry for 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Add the drained noodles, bean sprouts and cooked egg to the wok, tossing well to coat evenly.
Scatter with spring onion and serve immediately while hot.

Kung Pao Chicken.title: Kung Pao Chicken
method_steps: Rinse the basmati rice and cook according to packet directions until tender.
Cut the chicken thigh fillets into bite-sized pieces and toss with the cornflour to coat lightly.
Finely chop the garlic, grate the ginger, and slice the spring onion.
Combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar and brown sugar in a small bowl.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat with a little oil until just smoking.
Stir-fry the chicken for 5-6 minutes, until golden and cooked through, then remove from the wok.
Add the dried chillies, Sichuan peppercorns, garlic and ginger to the wok and stir-fry for 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the wok, pour in the sauce mixture, and toss to coat, cooking for 1-2 minutes, until the sauce thickens slightly.
Stir through the peanuts and half the spring onion and cook for 1 minute, until warmed through.
Divide the rice among bowls, top with the kung pao chicken, and scatter with the remaining spring onion to serve.

Sweet Chilli Chicken Stir Fry.title: Sweet Chilli Chicken Stir Fry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (300g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Slice the chicken breast (450g) into thin, even strips and finely chop the garlic (2 cloves).
Heat a splash of oil in a large wok over high heat.
Add the chicken and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through, then remove from the wok and set aside.
Add the mixed stir-fry vegetables (400g) and garlic to the wok and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until crisp-tender.
Return the chicken to the wok and add the sweet chilli sauce (3 tbsp) and soy sauce (1 tbsp).
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until well coated and heated through.
Serve immediately over the rice.

Ginger Beef Stir Fry.title: Ginger Beef Stir Fry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (300g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Thinly slice the red capsicum (1), finely grate the ginger (1 tbsp), finely chop the garlic (2 cloves), and slice the spring onion (3).
Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat with a little oil until very hot.
Add the beef strips (450g) in a single layer and fry for 2-3 minutes, turning occasionally, until well browned and crisp at the edges.
Add the capsicum and stir-fry for 2 minutes until just softened.
Add the ginger and garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the soy sauce (3 tbsp) and toss to coat everything evenly.
Stir through half the spring onion and cook for a further minute until the sauce is glossy and slightly reduced.
Serve immediately over the rice, scattered with the remaining spring onion.

Chicken Yakisoba.title: Chicken Yakisoba
method_steps: Slice the chicken thigh fillet (400g) into bite-sized pieces, finely shred the cabbage (1/4), julienne the carrot (1), and thinly slice the onion (1).
Loosen the hokkien noodles (400g) under warm water or according to packet instructions to separate the strands.
Heat oil in a large wok over high heat.
Add the chicken and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Add the onion, carrot and cabbage and stir-fry for 3 minutes until just softened.
Add the noodles to the wok.
Combine the Worcestershire sauce (2 tbsp), oyster sauce (1 tbsp) and soy sauce (1 tbsp), pour over the noodles, and toss for 2-3 minutes until evenly coated and heated through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapow).title: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapow)
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (250g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Finely chop the garlic (4 cloves) and long red chilli (2), and pick the basil leaves (1 bunch) from their stems.
Heat oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat.
Add the garlic and chilli and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chicken mince (500g) and stir-fry, breaking it up with a spoon, for 5-6 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Stir in the fish sauce (1 tbsp), oyster sauce (1 tbsp), soy sauce (1 tbsp) and brown sugar (1 tsp), and cook for a further minute.
Remove from the heat and stir through the basil leaves until just wilted.
Meanwhile, fry the eggs (4) in a separate pan until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft.
Spoon the basil chicken over the rice and top each serve with a fried egg.

Vietnamese Caramel Chicken.title: Vietnamese Caramel Chicken
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (300g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Cut the chicken thigh fillet (600g) into bite-sized pieces, finely chop the garlic (3 cloves), and thinly slice the cucumber (1).
Place the brown sugar (2 tbsp) in a dry saucepan or wok over medium heat.
Cook, swirling the pan occasionally, for 3-4 minutes until the sugar melts and turns a deep amber caramel.
Carefully add the fish sauce (2 tbsp) - it will bubble and steam briefly - and stir to combine.
Add the chicken and garlic to the pan and stir to coat in the caramel sauce.
Add a splash of water, then simmer over medium-low heat for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce is thick and sticky.
Season with black pepper (1/2 tsp) to taste.
Serve the chicken over the rice with the sliced cucumber on the side.

Japanese Chicken Curry.title: Japanese Chicken Curry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (300g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Cut the chicken thigh fillet (500g) into bite-sized pieces, and dice the onion (1), carrot (2) and potato (2).
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken and cook for 4-5 minutes until browned on all sides.
Add the onion, carrot and potato and cook for 3-4 minutes until the onion softens.
Pour in enough water to just cover the ingredients and bring to a simmer.
Simmer for 10-12 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Reduce the heat to low, add the curry roux (100g), and stir until fully dissolved.
Simmer for a further 5-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is thick and glossy and coats the back of a spoon.
Serve the curry over the rice.

Sticky Sesame Chicken.title: Sticky Sesame Chicken
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (300g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Cut the chicken breast (500g) into bite-sized pieces, trim the broccoli (1) into florets, and finely chop the garlic (2 cloves) and ginger (1 tsp).
Toss the chicken pieces in the cornflour (3 tbsp) until evenly coated.
Heat a generous layer of oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Fry the chicken in batches for 4-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and crisp on all sides, then drain on paper towel.
Steam or microwave the broccoli for 3-4 minutes until just tender.
In a small saucepan, combine the soy sauce (3 tbsp), honey (2 tbsp), garlic, ginger and sesame oil (1 tsp), and simmer for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened and sticky.
Return the chicken to the pan and toss through the sauce until well coated, then sprinkle with sesame seeds (1 tbsp).
Serve the sticky chicken over the rice with the broccoli alongside.

Beef Chow Fun.title: Beef Chow Fun
method_steps: Gently separate the fresh rice noodles (400g) with your hands to loosen the strands.
Heat oil in a large wok over high heat until smoking hot.
Add the beef strips (400g) and sear for 2-3 minutes until well browned, then remove and set aside.
Add a little more oil to the wok, then add the rice noodles and dark soy sauce (1 tsp), and stir-fry for 2 minutes until lightly coloured.
Add the bean sprouts (100g) and garlic chives (1 bunch) and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until just wilted.
Return the beef to the wok along with the soy sauce (2 tbsp) and oyster sauce (1 tbsp).
Toss everything together over high heat for 1-2 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Prawn Nasi Goreng.title: Prawn Nasi Goreng
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (300g) ahead of time and spread it out to cool completely - cold rice works best for fried rice.
Peel and devein the green prawns (300g), and finely chop the garlic (2 cloves), onion (1) and long red chilli (1).
Heat oil in a large wok or frying pan over high heat.
Add the prawns and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until pink and just cooked through, then remove and set aside.
Add the garlic, onion and chilli to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the frozen mixed vegetables (250g) and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until heated through and tender.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Return the prawns to the wok, add the kecap manis (3 tbsp), and toss until everything is evenly coated.
Meanwhile, fry the eggs (4) in a separate pan until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft.
Serve the nasi goreng topped with a fried egg.

Chilli Garlic Noodles with Chicken.title: Chilli Garlic Noodles with Chicken
method_steps: Bring a large pot of water to the boil and cook the egg noodles (400g) according to packet instructions, then drain.
Slice the chicken breast (400g) into thin strips, finely chop the garlic (3 cloves), julienne the carrot (1), and roughly chop the bok choy (2).
Heat oil in a large wok over high heat.
Add the chicken and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through, then remove and set aside.
Add the garlic and carrot to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the bok choy and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until just wilted.
Return the chicken to the wok along with the noodles, chilli garlic sauce (2 tbsp) and oyster sauce (1 tbsp).
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Serve immediately while hot.

Chicken Jalfrezi.title: Chicken Jalfrezi
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (200g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Cut the chicken thigh fillet (500g) into bite-sized pieces, slice the onion (1) and red capsicum (1), and finely chop the garlic (3 cloves) and ginger (1 tbsp).
Heat oil in a large frying pan or saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken and cook for 5-6 minutes until browned on all sides.
Add the onion and capsicum and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger, then stir in the curry powder (1 tbsp) and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned diced tomatoes (1 can) and stir to combine.
Simmer, uncovered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Season to taste and stir through the coriander (1/2 bunch), then serve with the rice.

Lamb Keema with Peas.title: Lamb Keema with Peas
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (200g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Finely dice the onion (1) and finely chop the garlic (3 cloves) and ginger (1 tbsp).
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat and cook the onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the lamb mince (500g) and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, for 5-6 minutes until browned.
Stir in the garam masala (1 tbsp) and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned diced tomatoes (1 can) and simmer, uncovered, for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the frozen peas (150g) and simmer for a further 5 minutes until tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve with the rice or roti.

Chana Aloo (Chickpea & Potato Curry).title: Chana Aloo (Chickpea & Potato Curry)
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (200g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Peel and dice the potatoes (3) into 2cm chunks, finely dice the onion (1), and finely chop the garlic (2 cloves) and ginger (1 tsp).
Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas (2 cans).
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger and stir in the curry powder (1 tbsp), cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the potato, chickpeas and tinned diced tomatoes (1 can), plus a splash of water to loosen the sauce.
Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the potato is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season to taste and serve with the rice.

Beef Madras.title: Beef Madras
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck (600g) into 3cm chunks and finely dice the onion (1) and garlic (3 cloves).
Heat oil in a large heavy-based saucepan or pot over medium-high heat.
Add the beef and brown for 5-6 minutes, turning occasionally, until well coloured on all sides.
Add the onion and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic and madras paste (3 tbsp) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the tinned diced tomatoes (1 can) and beef stock (200ml), stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beef is fork-tender.
Cook the basmati rice (200g) towards the end of the simmering time and keep warm.
Remove the lid and simmer the curry for a further 5-10 minutes if the sauce needs thickening.
Season to taste and serve with the rice.

Egg & Potato Curry.title: Egg & Potato Curry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (200g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Peel the potatoes (3) and cut into 2cm chunks.
Place the eggs (6) and potato in a saucepan of water, bring to the boil, and cook for 10-12 minutes until the eggs are hard-boiled and the potato is tender.
Drain, then peel the eggs and set aside with the potato.
Finely dice the onion (1) and finely chop the garlic (2 cloves) and ginger (1 tsp).
Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the onion, garlic and ginger for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the curry powder (1 tbsp) and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned diced tomatoes (1 can) and simmer for 8-10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Add the boiled eggs and potato to the sauce, halving the eggs if you like, and simmer for a further 3-4 minutes until heated through.
Serve with the rice.

Prawn Masala.title: Prawn Masala
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice (200g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Peel and devein the green prawns (500g), and finely dice the onion (1) and finely chop the garlic (2 cloves) and ginger (1 tsp).
Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic and ginger and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garam masala (1 tbsp) and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned diced tomatoes (1 can) and simmer for 10 minutes until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the prawns to the sauce and simmer for 6-8 minutes until they turn pink and are just cooked through.
Serve with the rice.

Chicken Tinga Tacos.title: Chicken Tinga Tacos
method_steps: Finely dice the onion (1) and finely chop the garlic (2 cloves).
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook the onion and garlic for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the chicken thigh fillet (500g), chipotle sauce (2 tbsp) and tinned diced tomatoes (1 can).
Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 20-25 minutes, turning the chicken occasionally, until it is cooked through and tender enough to shred easily.
Remove the chicken from the sauce and shred it with two forks.
Return the shredded chicken to the sauce and stir to coat, simmering for a further 3-4 minutes until thickened.
Thinly slice the red onion (1/2).
Warm the tortillas (8) in a dry frying pan or microwave, then spoon the chicken tinga into the tortillas and top with the sliced red onion.

Beef Barbacoa Bowl.title: Beef Barbacoa Bowl
method_steps: Trim any excess fat from the beef chuck (700g) and finely chop the garlic (3 cloves).
Place the beef in a slow cooker or heavy-based pot with the garlic, chipotle sauce (2 tbsp), ground cumin (1 tbsp) and dried oregano (1 tsp).
Add enough water to come halfway up the beef, then cover.
Cook on low for about 2 hours (or simmer gently on the stovetop, covered, over low heat) until the beef is completely tender and shreds easily with a fork.
Towards the end of cooking, prepare the basmati rice (250g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Warm the tinned black beans (1 can) in a small saucepan over low heat.
Remove the beef from the pot and shred it with two forks, discarding any excess fat.
Return the shredded beef to the cooking liquid, squeeze in the lime (1) juice, and toss to coat.
Serve the beef barbacoa over the rice with the black beans spooned alongside.

Pork Verde.title: Pork Verde
method_steps: Trim any excess fat from the pork shoulder (700g) and cut into large chunks, and finely dice the onion (1) and garlic (3 cloves).
Place the pork in a heavy-based pot or slow cooker with the onion, garlic, ground cumin (1 tsp) and salsa verde (200g).
Cover and cook on low for about 1.5 hours (or simmer gently on the stovetop over low heat) until the pork is completely tender and shreds easily with a fork.
Remove the pork from the pot and shred it with two forks, discarding any excess fat.
Return the shredded pork to the sauce and stir to coat, simmering for a further 5 minutes.
Warm the tortillas (8) in a dry frying pan or microwave.
Cut the lime (1) into wedges.
Serve the pork verde in the warmed tortillas with a squeeze of lime.

Black Bean & Sweet Potato Bowl.title: Black Bean & Sweet Potato Bowl
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray with baking paper.
Peel the sweet potato (600g) and cut into 2cm cubes.
Combine the ground cumin (1 tsp), smoked paprika (1 tsp), garlic powder (1/2 tsp), dried oregano (1/2 tsp) and cayenne pepper (1/4 tsp) in a small bowl.
Toss the sweet potato with a little oil and the spice mix until evenly coated.
Spread the sweet potato in a single layer on the tray and roast for 25-30 minutes, turning halfway, until tender and golden at the edges.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice (250g) according to packet instructions and keep warm.
Warm the tinned black beans (1 can) in a small saucepan over low heat.
Slice the avocado (1) and cut the lime (1) into wedges.
Divide the rice among bowls and top with the roasted sweet potato, black beans, sliced avocado and a squeeze of lime.

Chipotle Chicken Rice.title: Chipotle Chicken Rice
method_steps: Dice the chicken thigh fillets and finely chop the onion.
Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken and onion, and brown for 5-6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is golden and the onion has softened.
Stir in the chipotle sauce and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the basmati rice, diced tomatoes and chicken stock, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat for 18-20 minutes, until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove from the heat and rest, covered, for 5 minutes to let the rice finish steaming.
Fluff with a fork and serve hot.

Harissa Chicken Traybake.title: Harissa Chicken Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large roasting tray with baking paper.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas, and roughly chop the red capsicum and red onion into bite-sized pieces.
Place the chicken thigh cutlets, chickpeas, capsicum and onion in the tray.
Add the harissa paste and olive oil, then toss well until everything is evenly coated.
Arrange the chicken skin-side up in a single layer for even browning.
Roast for 40 minutes, turning the vegetables halfway, until the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C) and the skin is crisp and deeply golden.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve straight from the tray, spooning the harissa juices over the top.

Lemon Herb Fish Traybake.title: Lemon Herb Fish Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large baking tray with baking paper.
Halve the baby potatoes, trim the green beans, halve the cherry tomatoes, thinly slice the garlic, and cut the lemon into wedges.
Toss the potatoes, green beans, cherry tomatoes, garlic, dried oregano and olive oil together on the tray.
Roast for 15 minutes to begin softening the vegetables.
Remove the tray and nestle the fish fillets among the vegetables, season with salt and pepper, and tuck in the lemon wedges.
Return to the oven and roast for a further 15-18 minutes, until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Squeeze the roasted lemon wedges over the fish just before serving.
Serve immediately, spooning the pan juices over the top.

Moroccan Chickpea Stew.title: Moroccan Chickpea Stew
method_steps: Finely chop the onion and garlic, and cut the pumpkin into 2cm cubes.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat, and cook the onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, cumin, ground coriander, cinnamon, turmeric and paprika, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the chickpeas, diced tomatoes and pumpkin, along with a splash of water if needed.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pumpkin is tender and the stew has thickened.
Meanwhile, prepare the couscous according to the packet instructions.
Season the stew to taste with salt, and scatter with fresh coriander.
Serve the stew spooned over the couscous.

Spiced Lamb Flatbreads.title: Spiced Lamb Flatbreads
method_steps: Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat, and cook the onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the lamb mince, garlic, cumin and cinnamon, breaking up the mince with a spoon, and cook for 8-10 minutes until well browned and cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Warm the flatbreads in a dry pan or under the grill for about 1 minute per side.
Spread the spiced lamb mince over each flatbread.
Top with mixed salad leaves, cherry tomatoes and sliced cucumber.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon juice before serving.

Greek Chicken Skewers with Rice.title: Greek Chicken Skewers with Rice
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillets into even 3cm cubes.
In a bowl, combine the chicken with the juice and zest of the lemon, garlic, dried oregano, olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper, then marinate for at least 10 minutes.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Heat a chargrill pan or barbecue to medium-high heat.
Grill the skewers for 12-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until charred in spots and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C).
Thinly slice the cucumber.
Serve the skewers over the rice, scattered with the sliced cucumber.

Sausage & Bean Casserole.title: Sausage & Bean Casserole
method_steps: Finely chop the onion and garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based pot or casserole dish over medium-high heat.
Add the sausages and brown for 5-6 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden all over, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot, and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Return the sausages to the pot, then add the mixed beans, diced tomatoes and chicken stock.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sausages are cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve hot, spooned into bowls.

Chicken & Vegetable Traybake.title: Chicken & Vegetable Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large roasting tray with baking paper.
Cut the potatoes and carrots into even chunks, and slice the zucchini into thick rounds.
Place the chicken thigh cutlets and vegetables in the tray.
Drizzle with the olive oil and season with dried oregano, dried thyme, salt and pepper, then toss to coat evenly.
Arrange the chicken skin-side up in a single layer.
Roast for 40 minutes, turning the vegetables halfway through, until the chicken skin is golden and crisp and the potatoes are tender.
Check the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C) before removing from the oven.
Rest for 5 minutes, then serve straight from the tray.

Beef & Vegetable Stew.title: Beef & Vegetable Stew
method_steps: Cut the beef chuck into 3-4cm chunks and toss in the plain flour; roughly chop the onion, carrot and celery.
Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based pot over medium-high heat.
Add the beef in batches and brown well on all sides, about 6-8 minutes per batch, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion, carrot and celery to the pot, and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, then return the beef to the pot.
Pour in the beef stock, scraping up any browned bits from the base, and bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook on low heat for 1.5 hours.
Add the potato, cut into chunks, and continue simmering for a further 30 minutes until the beef is fork-tender and the potato is cooked through; season to taste with salt and pepper.

Pork & Apple Traybake.title: Pork & Apple Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large roasting tray with baking paper.
Cut the potatoes into chunks, and the apples and red onion into wedges.
Place the potato in the tray with half the olive oil, and roast for 15 minutes to give it a head start.
Add the pork loin steaks, apple and red onion to the tray.
Season with dried sage, salt and pepper, and drizzle with the remaining olive oil.
Roast for a further 25-30 minutes, until the pork is cooked through (internal temperature reaches 70°C) and the apple and potato are tender and golden.
Rest the pork for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the pork with the roasted apple, onion and potato, spooning over any pan juices.

Lemon Herb Chicken Drumsticks.title: Lemon Herb Chicken Drumsticks
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a roasting tray with baking paper.
Finely chop or crush the garlic, and zest and juice the lemon.
In a large bowl, combine the drumsticks with the lemon juice, zest, garlic, dried oregano, olive oil, and salt and pepper.
Toss well until the drumsticks are evenly coated, then arrange in a single layer on the tray.
Roast for 40 minutes, turning halfway through, until the skin is golden and crisp and the chicken is cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C).
Rest the drumsticks for 5 minutes.
Serve with the mixed salad leaves alongside.

Fish & Chickpea Traybake.title: Fish & Chickpea Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large baking tray with baking paper.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas, halve the cherry tomatoes, and chop the red capsicum into bite-sized pieces.
Place the chickpeas, cherry tomatoes and capsicum in the tray, drizzle with olive oil, and season with the cumin and smoked paprika.
Toss to coat, then roast for 15 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften.
Remove the tray and nestle the fish fillets among the vegetables, seasoning the fish with salt and pepper.
Return to the oven and roast for a further 15 minutes, until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Squeeze the lemon over the fish and vegetables just before serving.
Serve immediately, spooning the tray juices over the top.

Beef Mince & Vegetable Skillet.title: Beef Mince & Vegetable Skillet
method_steps: Finely chop the onion and garlic, and cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and garlic, and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add the beef mince, breaking it up with a spoon, and cook for 5-6 minutes until well browned.
Stir in the frozen mixed vegetables and tomato passata.
Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve the mince spooned over the rice.

Chicken & Chorizo Traybake.title: Chicken & Chorizo Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a large roasting tray with baking paper.
Cut the potatoes into chunks, slice the chorizo, and chop the red capsicum into bite-sized pieces.
Place the chicken thigh cutlets, chorizo, potato and capsicum in the tray.
Sprinkle with smoked paprika, drizzle with olive oil, and toss well to coat evenly.
Arrange the chicken skin-side up in a single layer.
Roast for 40 minutes, turning the vegetables halfway, until the chicken is golden and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C) and the potato is tender.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve straight from the tray, spooning over the paprika-spiced chorizo oil.

Lentil Bolognese.title: Lentil Bolognese
method_steps: Finely chop the onion, carrot, celery and garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat, and cook the onion, carrot and celery for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Drain and rinse the brown lentils, then stir them into the pan with the tomato paste, dried oregano, dried basil and tomato passata.
Bring to a simmer, then cook uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and coats the back of a spoon.
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti in a large pot of salted boiling water according to the packet instructions.
Season the lentil sauce to taste with salt and pepper.
Drain the spaghetti and toss through the sauce, then serve immediately.

Vegetable Paella.title: Vegetable Paella
method_steps: Finely chop the onion, red capsicum and garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large, wide paella pan or frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and capsicum, and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
