Add the garlic and smoked paprika, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the rice, coating it well in the paprika oil, then pour in the vegetable stock.
Bring to a simmer, then cook without stirring for 15-18 minutes, until most of the liquid is absorbed and the rice is nearly tender.
Scatter the frozen peas over the top and cook for a further 5 minutes, until the rice is tender and a golden crust forms on the base.
Rest for 2-3 minutes off the heat before serving straight from the pan.

Prawn Paella.title: Prawn Paella
method_steps: Peel and devein the prawns if needed, and finely chop the onion and red capsicum.
Heat the olive oil in a large, wide paella pan or frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and capsicum, and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the rice, stirring to coat, then pour in the chicken stock.
Bring to a simmer, then cook without stirring for 15 minutes, until most of the liquid is absorbed.
Scatter the prawns and frozen peas over the rice, and cook for a further 5 minutes, until the prawns are pink, curled and cooked through.
Rest for 2-3 minutes off the heat, then serve straight from the pan.

Sausage & Lentil Stew.title: Sausage & Lentil Stew
method_steps: Finely chop the onion and carrot.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add the sausages and brown for 5-6 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden all over, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion and carrot to the pot, and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Drain and rinse the brown lentils, then add them to the pot along with the diced tomatoes and chicken stock.
Slice the browned sausages and return them to the pot.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sausages are cooked through and the stew has thickened.
Season to taste with salt and pepper before serving.

Pumpkin & Lentil Dahl.title: Pumpkin & Lentil Dahl
method_steps: Finely chop the onion and garlic, grate the ginger, and cut the pumpkin into 2cm cubes.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat, and cook the onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, ginger, garam masala and turmeric, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Rinse the red lentils, then add them to the pan with the pumpkin and enough water or stock to cover.
Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils have broken down and the pumpkin is tender.
Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions.
Season the dahl to taste with salt.
Serve the dahl spooned over the rice.

Teriyaki Beef Bowl.title: Teriyaki Beef Bowl
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions, and cut the broccoli into small florets.
Steam or microwave the broccoli for 3-4 minutes until just tender and bright green.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat until very hot.
Add the beef strips, garlic and ginger in a single layer, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned but still tender.
Pour in the teriyaki sauce and toss to coat the beef evenly, cooking for a further 1-2 minutes until glossy.
Serve the beef over the rice, with the steamed broccoli alongside.
Scatter with sesame seeds to finish.

Moroccan Chicken Traybake.title: Moroccan Chicken Traybake
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (fan-forced) and line a large roasting tray with baking paper.
Peel and cut the sweet potato into 2cm cubes, drain and rinse the chickpeas, and cut the red onion into thin wedges.
In a small bowl, mix the ground cumin, ground coriander, sweet paprika, cinnamon, ground ginger, turmeric and cayenne pepper.
In a large bowl, toss the chicken thigh cutlets, sweet potato, chickpeas, red onion, minced garlic and olive oil with the spice mix until everything is well coated.
Spread the mixture out in a single layer over the tray, arranging the chicken skin-side up.
Roast for 35-40 minutes, turning the vegetables halfway through, until the chicken is golden and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 75°C) and the sweet potato is tender.
Drizzle the honey and lemon juice over the tray for the final 5 minutes of roasting.
Rest the chicken for a few minutes, scatter with fresh coriander leaves and serve straight from the tray.

Sweet & Sticky Pork Stir Fry.title: Sweet & Sticky Pork Stir Fry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions and keep warm.
Pat the pork stir-fry strips dry with paper towel to help them brown well.
Heat a splash of oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat until just shimmering.
Stir-fry the pork in batches for 2-3 minutes until browned but not fully cooked through, then remove to a plate.
Add the garlic and ginger to the wok and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the mixed stir-fry vegetables and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until bright and crisp-tender.
Return the pork to the wok with the hoisin sauce, soy sauce, honey and sesame oil, and toss for 1-2 minutes until the pork is cooked through and coated in a glossy, sticky sauce.
Serve immediately over the warm rice, scattered with sliced spring onion.

Cajun Chicken & Rice.title: Cajun Chicken & Rice
method_steps: Cut the chicken thigh fillet into bite-sized pieces.
Mix the smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, dried thyme, cayenne and black pepper in a small bowl, then toss with the chicken to coat.
Finely dice the onion and red capsicum.
Heat the olive oil in a large, deep pan over medium-high heat and brown the chicken for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the onion and capsicum and cook for 3 minutes, stirring, until softened and fragrant.
Stir in the basmati rice, coating the grains in the spiced juices.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to the boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove from the heat and rest, covered, for 5 minutes to let the rice finish steaming.
Fluff with a fork and serve hot.

Chickpea & Spinach Curry.title: Chickpea & Spinach Curry
method_steps: Cook the basmati rice according to the packet instructions and keep warm.
Finely dice the onion, and mince the garlic and ginger.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and fry the onion for about 4 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, ginger and curry paste and fry for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Stir in the chickpeas (with their liquid), diced tomatoes and coconut milk, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir through the baby spinach and cook for a further 2 minutes until just wilted.
Season to taste and serve hot over the rice.

Beef & Broccoli Noodle Bowl.title: Beef & Broccoli Noodle Bowl
method_steps: Cook the egg noodles according to the packet instructions, then drain and set aside.
Cut the broccoli into small florets, and mince the garlic and ginger.
Mix the soy sauce, oyster sauce, cornflour, brown sugar and 60ml water in a small bowl to make the sauce.
Heat a little oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry the beef strips in batches for 2-3 minutes until browned, then remove to a plate.
Add the garlic and ginger to the wok and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the broccoli and stir-fry for about 3 minutes until bright green and crisp-tender, adding a small splash of water to help it steam if needed.
Return the beef to the wok, pour in the sauce mixture and toss for 1-2 minutes until it thickens and coats everything.
Add the cooked noodles and sesame oil, tossing until well coated and heated through, then serve immediately.
